May not be included: When determining in the HH family, the CAO must not include the following household members: - The HH client's spouse with TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - Siblings of a child who is an HH applicant/recipient. - The parent of an HH child who is eligible for a waiver using the special income limit of 300 percent of the Federal Benefit Rate and who has services under a HCBS Program.

title: 310 Appendix B
section_title: Categorically Needy Nonmoney Payment (NMP for the Family)
text: Must be included: When determining the NMP for the Family (NMPF) applicant/recipient group , the CAO must include the following household members and count their income: - Biological or adoptive parents of a deprived child (including unborn children). - A sibling of the deprived child, if the sibling is related by blood and the sibling is eligible because of age and deprivation. Exceptions: An individual is not included if he or she: - Has SSI or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - Has federal IV-E or state or local foster care assistance or adoption assistance. NOTE: The individual is not included as a member of a TANF budget group unless the child is receiving federal, state, or local adoption assistance and including the child in the budget group would increase the benefits for the budget group. If including the adopted child would increase the benefits, then the child must be included in the budget group. The childâs income from the federal, state, or local subsidies is counted in the eligibility and benefit determinations. (See Cash Assistance Handbook, Chapter 150, Income .) - Is ineligible because he or she is not a U.S. citizen , a resident alien , or an eligible refugee ; or - Is ineligible for any other reason. NOTE: When the individual becomes eligible, he or she must be included in the applicant/recipient group. May be included: When determining the NMPF family, the CAO may include the following other individuals who live with the child: - One individual who is designated as the specified relative of the TANF child but is not the parent. - An individual related to the TANF child who would be eligible for GA if they applied. - A child with federal IV-E or state or local foster care assistance or adoption assistance is not included unless including the child and the income in the budget group would make the family eligible.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: It is important not to confuse the applicant/recipient group with the Healthy Beginnings family. The Healthy Beginnings family includes those household members whose income is counted when determining eligibility. An applicant/recipient group for HB may include any woman who is pregnant and any qualified child if the pregnant woman and qualified child are related by blood or marriage to at least one member of the applicant/recipient group. Must be included: When determining the HB applicant/recipient group, the CAO must include the following household members and count their income: - The husband (including a common-law husband) of a pregnant woman. - Parents of a pregnant woman if the pregnant woman is unemancipated and: - Age 18 or older and in school. NOTE: For Healthy Beginnings, a individual is emancipated if he or she is: - Under age 19 and either married or no longer under the care or control of a parent. - The biological or adoptive parent of a qualified child. - A father who has legally been shown to be the parent of a qualified child. - Unborn children of pregnant woman. NOTE: The individuals who must be included must be living in the same household as the pregnant woman or the qualified child. - A sibling under age 21 (including the stepsibling or half-sibling of a pregnant woman) if the pregnant woman is under age 19, unemancipated and living in the parental household. Exception : A sibling under age 21 must not be included with the pregnant woman who is age 18 (whether emancipated or unemancipated) and not in school. May be included: When determining the HB applicant/recipient group, the CAO may include the following household members and count their income: - A putative father , if he lives with the pregnant woman and the pregnant woman chooses to include him as a family member.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: NOTE: When the child is born, the CAO must not include the putative father in the family size when determining the child's eligibility until paternity is legally proven. - A natural or adopted child or stepchild of a pregnant woman if the child is under age 21. - A sister, stepsister, or half-sister of a qualified child if the sister is under age 21. - A brother, stepbrother, or half-brother of a qualified child if the brother is under age 21. - An individual who takes care of and is in charge of an HB child. - A child under age 21 if the HB applicant/recipient is taking care of and in charge of the child. Must not be included: When determining the HB applicant/recipient group, the CAO must not include the following household members: - Any family member with cash assistance, SSI, NMP for the Family (PC/PU27), or PA/PJ/PM-85 (Special SSI Recipient Status). - A sister, stepsister, half-sister, brother, stepbrother, or half-brother under age 21 or a parent of a pregnant woman who lives in the parental household, if the pregnant woman is: - Age 18 and unemancipated but not expected to graduate from secondary or high school before age 19. - Age 18, unemancipated, and not in school. - Any other individual who is not identified as either "Must be included" or "May be included."

title: 310 Appendix B
text: Must be included: When determining the NMP applicant/recipient group, the CAO must include the following household members and count their income: - The spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse is applying for or receives SSI-related NMP. - A spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse receives HB or is eligible but not yet approved for HB. NOTE: The HB client's income is included and the NMP income limit is increased to include the HB spouse and the unborn child, but the HB spouse is not approved for NMP. - A parent of a TANF-related NMP applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent receives HB, TANF-related NMP, or GA-related NMP or is eligible but not yet approved for it. - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse receives HB or is eligible but not yet approved for HB. NOTE: The HB client's income is included and the NMP income limit is increased to include the HB parent or spouse and the unborn child, but the HB parent or spouse is not approved for NMP. - A sister, half-sister, brother, or half-brother under age 21 of an unemancipated child who is a TANF-related NMP applicant/recipient when the sister, half-sister, brother, or half-brother is applying for or has TANF-related NMP. - A child under age 21 of a TANF-related or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient when the child is applying for or receiving TANF-related NMP. Exception: An emancipated child or a stepchild is not required to be included in the same applicant/recipient group with his or her parent when applying for or receiving NMP. Income is deemed from a parent to a child under age 21, whether or not the child is emancipated.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: May be included: When determining the NMP applicant/recipient group, the CAO may include the following household members and count their income: - A stepsister or stepbrother of a child. - A child under 21 years of age if the NMP applicant/recipient is taking care of and in charge of the child. - Any individual related by blood or marriage. - A related individual or relative who is taking care of and in charge of a child under age 21. - A child under age 21 of a TANF-related, GA-related, or SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the child receives or is eligible but not yet approved for HB. NOTE: If the child is included in the eligibility determination for the remaining members of the household applying for or receiving TANF-related or GA-related NMP, the child's income must be included. The NMP income limit is increased to include the HB child, but the HB child is not approved for NMP. NOTE: If the child is included in the eligibility determination for the remaining members of the household applying for or receives SSI-related NMP, the child's income must be included. May not be included, but income is deemed: When determining the NMP applicant/recipient group, the CAO must not include the following household members, but the CAO must deem their income as available to the child or spouse: - A spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85) and the spouse's net available income is more than 50 percent of the federal benefit rate for SSI. NOTE: The nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse's income is included, and the NMP income limit is increased to include the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse, but the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse is not authorized for MA.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: - A spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive HH or has not been found eligible or approved for HH and the spouse's net available income is more than 50 percent of the federal benefit rate for SSI. NOTE: The nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse's income is included, and the NMP income limit is increased to include the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse, but the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse is not approved. - A parent of a TANF-related NMP applicant/recipient if the parent does not receive or has not been found eligible or approved for HB, HH, or SSI-related NMP. - A parent of a TANF-related NMP applicant/recipient if the parent does not receive TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM 85). - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive TANF, GA, NMP, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive or has not been found eligible or approved for HB, HH, or SSI-related NMP. May not be included: When determining the NMP applicant/recipient group, the CAO may not include the following household members and may not count their income: - A spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse's net available income is 50% or less of the federal benefit rate for SSI. - A spouse of an SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse receives TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - A parent of a TANF-related NMP applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent receives TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85).

title: 310 Appendix B
text: - A spouse of an SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse receives or is eligible but not yet approved for HH. - A spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse receives or is eligible but not yet approved for TANF-related or GA-related NMP. - A parent of a TANF-related NMP applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent receives or is eligible but not yet approved for HH or SSI-related NMP. - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse receives or is eligible but not yet approved for SSI-related NMP. - A parent of a child under age 21 if the child is an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient. - The child of an SSI-related applicant or recipient. - The spouse or parent of an SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient if the spouse or parent is eligible for a waiver using the special income limit of 300 percent of the federal benefit rate and has services under an HCBS program.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: Must be included: When determining the MNO applicant/recipient group, the CAO must include the following household members and count their income: - A spouse of an SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse is applying for or receiving SSI-related MNO. - A parent of a TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent is applying for or receiving TANF-related or GA-related MNO. - A parent of an SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient under age 18 if the parent is applying for or receiving TANF-related or GA-related MNO. - A spouse of an SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse receiving or is eligible but not yet approved for HB. NOTE: The HB client's income is included, and the MNO income limit is increased to include the HB MA spouse and any unborn children, but the HB spouse is not approved for MNO. - A child under age 21 of a TANF-related, GA-related, or SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient when the child is applying for or receiving TANF-related MNO. Exception: An emancipated child and a stepchild do not need to be included in the same applicant/recipient group with his or her parent or stepparent when applying for or receiving MNO. Income will be deemed from a parent to a child under 18 whether or not the child is emancipated. - A sister, half-sister, brother, or half-brother under age 21 of an unemancipated child who is a TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient when the sister, half-sister, brother, or half-brother is applying for or receiving MNO benefits. - A parent of a TANF-related applicant/recipient under 18 years of age if the parent receives or is eligible but not yet approved for HB. NOTE: The HB income limit is increased to count the HB parent and any unborn children, but the HB parent is not authorized MNO.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: May be included: When determining the MNO applicant/recipient group, the CAO may include the following household members and count their income: - A stepsister, half-sister, stepbrother, or half-brother of a child under age 21. - An individual who takes care of and is in charge of a child under age 21. - A child under 21 years of age if the MNO applicant/recipient is taking care of and in charge of the child. - A child under age 21 of a TANF-related, GA-related, or SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient if the child receives or is eligible but not yet approved for HB. NOTE: If the child is included as a member in the applicant/recipient group for MNO, the child's income must be included. The MNO income limit is increased to include the HB child, but the HB child is not approved for MNO. May not be included, but income is deemed: When determining the MNO applicant/recipient group, the CAO may not include the following household members, but the CAO deems their income as available to the child or spouse: - A parent of a TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient child under age 18 who does not receive TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - A spouse of an SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM 85) and the spouse's net available income is more than 50 percent of the federal benefit rate for SSI. - The nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse's income is included, and the MNO income limit is increased to include the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse, but the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse is not authorized. - A spouse of an SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive HH or has not been found eligible or approved for HH and the spouse's net available income is more than 50 percent of the federal benefit rate for SSI.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: NOTE: The nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse's income is included, and the MNO income limit is increased to include the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse, but the nonapplicant/nonrecipient spouse is not authorized. - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse does not receive TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - NOTE: An unborn child is included in determining the needs of a pregnant nonapplicant spouse. - A parent of a TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient if the parent does not receive HB or HH or has not been found eligible or approved for HB or HH. - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse is not receiving HB or HH or has not been found eligible or approved for HB or HH. Not included: When determining the MNO applicant/recipient group, the CAO may not include the following household members and must not count their income: - A spouse of an SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse's net available income is 50 percent or less of the federal benefit rate for SSI. - A spouse of an SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse receives TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - A parent of a TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent receives TANF, GA, SBP, SSI, or MA with Special SSI Recipient Status (PA/PJ/PM-85). - A spouse of an SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse receives or is eligible but not yet authorized for HH. - A parent of an SSI-related or TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent receives or is eligible but not yet authorized for HH.

title: 310 Appendix B
text: - A spouse of a TANF-related or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse receives or is eligible but not yet authorized for SSI-related NMP. - A parent of a child under age 21 if the child is an SSI-related MNO applicant/recipient. - A parent of a TANF-related or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient under age 21 if the parent receives or is eligible but not approved for SSI-related NMP. - The spouse or parent of an SSI-related, TANF-related or GA-related MNO applicant/recipient if the spouse or parent is eligible for a waiver using the special income limit of 300 percent of the federal benefit rate and receives services under an HCBS program.

title: 310.3 Individuals Who May Be Included in the Same or Separate Applicant/Recipient Group
section_title: 310.3 Individuals Who May Be Included in the Same or Separate Applicant/Recipient Group
text: Relatives who are not members of the immediate family may be included in the same applicant/recipient group or may receive MA in a separate applicant/recipient group. 55 Pa. Code § 181.2 55 Pa. Code § 178.2 A stepchild or stepsibling who is under age 21 and unemancipated may be in a separate applicant/recipient group or may be included in the same applicant/recipient group with his or her stepparent or stepsibling. If the child's biological or adoptive parent also lives in the household and is applying for or receiving MA, the child is included in the parent's applicant/recipient group. The family can determine which option best meets their medical needs.

title: 310.3 Individuals Who May Be Included in the Same or Separate Applicant/Recipient Group
text: 55 Pa. Code § 181.2 55 Pa. Code § 178.2 If MA benefits are received in separate applicant/recipient groups, the eligibility of each group for either NMP or MNO is determined separately. - Mrs. M. applies for MA for herself and for her stepson, Juan, age 16. Both have income from employment. Her husband, Juan's father, deserted the family last week. The CAO determines that Mrs. M. and Juan will not be eligible for MA if they are included in the same applicant/recipient group but will be eligible in two separate applicant/recipient groups: TC for Mrs. M, and PC for Juan. - The family consists of Mr. and Mrs. C., Mr. C.'s daughter from a previous marriage, and Mrs. C.'s son from a previous marriage. - If Mr. and Mrs. C. apply only for either or both of the children, each child may be in their own applicant/recipient group. - If Mr. C. applies for himself, his daughter, and his stepson and Mrs. C. does not apply, Mr. C. must be included in the same applicant/recipient group as his daughter. His stepson may be in a separate group. - If Mrs. C. applies for herself, her son, and her stepdaughter and Mr. C. does not apply, Mrs. C must be included in the same applicant/recipient group as her son. Her stepdaughter may be in a separate group. - If Mr. and Mrs. C. apply for themselves and both of the children, all must be in one applicant/recipient group. (Spouses, parents, and children must belong to the same group.) - Mr. and Mrs. D. apply for MA for themselves, his 17-year-old daughter from a previous marriage, and her 16-year-old son from a previous marriage. Mrs. D. is not a U.S. citizen and is unable to provide acceptable papers showing her alien status. She is not eligible for MA. The CAO authorizes MA for Mr. D. and his daughter in one applicant/recipient group and MA for Mrs. D.'s son in a separate applicant/recipient group. Three months later, Mrs. D. provides proof of eligible alien status and requests MA. The CAO authorizes MA for all four members of the D. family in one applicant/recipient group.

title: 310.3 Individuals Who May Be Included in the Same or Separate Applicant/Recipient Group
text: Other related individuals who live together, but are not members of the immediate family may be in their own separate applicant/recipient group or may be included with their relatives. Such relationships include, but are not limited to, an aunt living with a niece, two adult brothers living together, or a 10-year-old child living with her grandmother. Bertha W., age 55, lives with her 50-year-old brother, Ernest. Neither is disabled , and neither meets the criteria for an SSI -related category . Bertha and Ernest can be in a single two-person applicant/recipient group, or they can be in two separate one-person applicant/recipient groups. The CAO will tell them about the choices and let them know how each choice would affect their eligibility. Mrs. McC. applies for MA for herself, her husband, their two children, and her 7-year-old nephew, Jack, who lives with the family. Jack receives Social Security benefits on the claim of his father, who has died. Jack can be included in the same applicant/recipient group with his aunt, uncle, and cousins (a five-person applicant/recipient group), or he can be in a separate, one-person applicant/recipient group. His aunt, uncle, and cousins would then be in a four-person applicant/recipient group.

title: 310.3 Individuals Who May Be Included in the Same or Separate Applicant/Recipient Group
text: An individual must apply for and receive MA in a separate applicant/recipient group if he or she is not related to another individual who is living in the household. Unrelated individuals may not choose to be included in the applicant/recipient group of other members of the household. Violet E., age 56, and Guy M., age 60, apply for MA. They have lived together for six years but are not married. Each will receive MA in a separate applicant/recipient group. Joy L. applies for MA for herself, her son Jeremy, and her boyfriend, Jason E. Mr. E. says he is the father of Jeremy, but paternity has not been proven. Mr. E. is considered unrelated to the other household members. So, he is in a separate applicant/recipient group. Lucy J, age 30 is caring for her best friendâs three year old daughter. The mother of the child (best friend) is incarcerated. Lucy is exercising care and control of the three year old. Lucy J is applying for MA for herself and the three year old. Since Lucy J is unrelated to the three year old, each will receive MA in a separate applicant/recipient group.

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: A household may have more than one applicant/recipient group . The CAO will determine income and resource eligibility separately for each applicant/recipient group. NOTE: NMP for the Family (NMPF, PC/PU 27) eligibility must be determined for the whole family before looking at eligibility for any one individual. A family member who is eligible for NMPF is not included in a Healthy Beginnings , TANF / GA -related NMP, or TANF/GA/ SSI -related MNO applicant/recipient groups. Individuals eligible for NMPF are not included in the eligibility decisions for these applicant/recipient groups. Their income is not counted or deemed to these applicant/recipient groups. Each individual (except for spouses) is a separate applicant/recipient group. Spouses who are both eligible for HH are a two-member applicant/recipient group.

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: Example: Chris J., age 65, and his brother, Bill, age 67, are applying for HH. Each is a separate applicant/recipient group. The income and resources of each brother has no effect on the eligibility of the other. A household may have one or more HH applicant/recipient groups and an NMP, MNO, or HB applicant/recipient group. The CAO will not count the income and resources of the HH applicant/recipient when determining eligibility of other household members for NMP or MNO. - This rule does not apply for NMPF determination or to individuals who have or are eligible for Healthy Horizons Cost-Sharing (PG), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB) , or Qualifying Individuals Group 1 (QI-1). - In determining eligibility for Healthy Beginnings, the CAO must count all mandatory family members who receive or who are eligible for Healthy Horizons. - An individual who receives or is eligible for but not yet approved for the PH-20 category must be considered if the individual is an LRR to the other people. If the entire household, including the PH-20 member, is eligible for TANF-related NMP or SSI-related NMP, then the PH-20 member is converted to the NMP categor y. If the entire household, including the PH-20 member, is eligible for TANF-related MNO or SSI-related MNO, then the PH-20 member remains a PH-20 category. The spouse or parent (biological or adoptive) of the HH applicant/recipient is included in the eligibility determination for HH, regardless of whether he or she receives or is eligible for but not yet approved for any other coverage group of MA . The countable income and net income of the spouse or parent is counted according to the rules in Chapter 319, Healthy Horizons . The spouse or parent must be included.

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: NOTE: The unborn child of an HH applicant/recipient or spouse must be counted as a member of the applicant/recipient group when determining the right income limit. For the child of the HH applicant/recipient who is included in the HH eligibility determination, regardless of whether he or she receives or is eligible for but not yet approved for any other coverage group of MA, income is counted according to the TANF-related or GA-related rules in Chapter 350, Income . However, the income deductions of the child or parent are counted according to the rules in Chapter 319, Healthy Horizons . The inclusion of a child is optional. When determining MA eligibility for other household members, the CAO will treat the income and resources of the PH 20 member as follows : - If the entire household, including the PH 20 member, is eligible for NMP, all household income is counted. The PH 20 member is converted to the NMP category, unless the NMP category is GA-related. In that case, the PH 20 member remains a PH 20 and all other members are NMP GA-related. - If the entire household, including the PH 20 member, is eligible for MNO, all household income is counted. The PH 20 member remains PH 20 and the other members are MNO. - If any members of the household are eligible for the Healthy Beginnings program, the income of the PH 20 member is counted. The PH 20 member remains PH 20.

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: Immediate family members eligible for HB are included in each applicant/recipient group as either eligible members or counted members. (See Chapter 318.2, Who Is In the Healthy Beginnings Family? .) 55 Pa. Code § 140.1 Reminder: The income of an individual who has HB or is eligible for but not yet approved for HB must be considered if that individual is an LRR to the other individual applying for or receiving NMP or MNO. Other HB applicants/recipients who are related by blood or marriage to at least one member of the NMP or MNO applicant/recipient group may be included in the MA eligibility determination. The spouse or parent of an HB applicant/recipient must be included in the HB eligibility determination, regardless of whether the spouse or parent is receiving or is eligible for but not yet approved for any other coverage group of MA. The income of the spouse or parent is treated according to the TANF-related or GA-related rules in Chapter 350, Income . 55 Pa. Code § 181.2 55 Pa. Code § 178.2 Example: Jane, age 20, is pregnant and is applying for HB. Her husband, David, age 20, is applying for TANF-related NMP. Jane and David are two separate applicant/recipient groups. When determining Jane's HB eligibility, David is included as a family member and his income is considered. A three-person income limit for HB is used. When determining David's eligibility for TANF-related NMP, Jane and the unborn child are included in the eligibility determination for NMP. A three-person NMP income limit is used when determining income eligibility, but NMP is approved only for David, if eligible.

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: Separate applicant/recipient groups are set up for SSI-related and TANF-related or GA-related benefits. Reminder: Eligibility must be considered for NMP for the Family (NMPF) as a single budget group before determining the eligibility of one individual. Each individual, other than spouses, is a separate applicant/recipient group. Spouses who are both eligible for an SSI-related category are one applicant/recipient group. The CAO will not count the income and resources of the PA, PJ, or PM recipient or of an applicant who is eligible but not yet approved when determining eligibility of other household members for NMP or MNO. A child of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient is not included in the eligibility determination, whether or not the child is applying for or receiving MA. The spouse of an SSI-related NMP applicant/recipient is included in the eligibility determination if the spouse is not elderly or disabled and is not applying for or receiving MA. The income of the spouse who is not elderly or disabled is deemed if it is more than 50 percent of the federal benefit rate for SSI. (See Chapter 355, Deeming Income and Resources .) Members of an immediate family who are applying for a TANF-related or GA-related category are included in each applicant/recipient group as either an eligible member or a counted member. Relatives who are not immediate family members may be included or may be a separate applicant/recipient group. Unrelated individuals will always be a separate applicant/recipient group. The spouse of the NMP applicant/recipient who does not receive or is not eligible for any other coverage group of MA is included in the eligibility decision for NMP. The income of the spouse is deemed. (See Chapter 355, Deeming Income and Resources .)

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: 55 Pa. Code § 181.110(e) A spouse, parent, or child of an NMP applicant/recipient who receives HH or is eligible for but not yet approved for HH, is not included in the eligibility determination for NMP. A spouse, parent, or child of a TANF-related or GA-related NMP-MA applicant/recipient who is receiving HB or is eligible but not yet approved for HB, is treated according to the TANF-related or GA-related rules. (See Chapter 350, Income .) The spouse or parent must be included. The children may be included. When HB clients are included in the eligibility determination for NMP-MA, the income limit is increased according to the number of HB clients, including any unborn children. Example: The household is made up of a disabled husband, his wife, and three children from her previous marriage. The husband receives SSD benefits. The wife has no income. The children receive child support. (See CAH 131). The husband is found eligible for Healthy Horizons. The children are found eligible for Healthy Beginnings. The husband and his income are not counted in the NMP eligibility determination for his wife. The children are optional counted members in their motherâs NMP eligibility determination. The mother is approved for PC 00. A spouse, parent, or child of the TANF-related or GA-related NMP applicant/recipient who receives HB or is eligible for but not yet approved for HB is treated according to the TANF-related or GA-related rules in Chapter 350, Income . The spouse or parent must be included. The children may be included. When HB clients are included in the eligibility decision for NMP, the income limit is increased according to the number of HB clients included.

title: 310.4 Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: All applying members of the immediate family are included in each applicant/recipient group, regardless of category, either as eligible members or as counted members. The spouse, parent, or child of the TANF-related MNO applicant/recipient who is receiving HB or is eligible but not yet approved for HB, is treated according to the TANF-related or GA-related rules. (See Chapter 350, Income .) The spouse or parent must be included. The children may be included. NOTE: If the members of the household include an LRR with HB benefits, the income of the LRR must be included. The income limit is increased according to the number of HB clients included in the MNO determination. Example: Mr. and Mrs. Y. and their two children, ages 14 and 16, apply for MA. Mrs. Y. is pregnant. HB is approved for Mrs. Y. as a one-member applicant/recipient group. Mrs. Y. and the unborn child are included in the determination of the correct income limit when determining eligibility for the applicant/recipient group that includes her husband and two children. 55 Pa. Code § 181.285(a)(1) NOTE: If the members of the household include an LRR or relative with HH or SSI-related NMP, the income and resources of the LRR or relative are not considered in the MNO eligibility determination for the other family members.

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: For a household of Healthy Horizons (HH) applicants/recipients and individuals who are applying for or receiving other MA coverage, the CAO will do the following: 1. Determine income eligibility of the HH applicant / recipient according to Chapter 319, Healthy Horizons, Section 319.8, Determining Eligibility . - Compute the countable income of the family members according to Chapter 319.6, Income . - Combine the countable gross earned and unearned income of the family members with the countable gross earned and unearned income of the HH applicant/recipients. Apply the deductions allowed under Chapter 319, Healthy Horizons, Section 319.7, Income Deductions . NOTE: An HH applicant who also meets the eligibility requirements for the HB program is included in the eligibility determination for the other household members who are applying for or receiving MA, depending on which income group benefits all the individuals in the household the most. An applicant/recipient who meets eligibility requirements for both HB and HH must choose one coverage group when deciding whether other family members are eligible. If the applicant/recipient chooses HH, the HH client is not included in the eligibility determination of the remaining members. If the applicant/recipient chooses HB, the HB client is treated according to the rules for the right coverage group for the other members. 2. Determine whether the HH applicant/recipient and spouse are eligible based on their resources according to Chapter 319, NOTE: There is no consideration of resources for individuals in an HH applicant/recipient group if: - The individual is a child under age 21. - The HH applicant/recipient is living in the household with a spouse under age 21. - The HH applicant/recipient is living with an adopted or natural child under age 21. - The HH applicant/recipient is living with a stepchild under age 21.

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: - The HH applicant/recipient is living with, taking care of, and in charge of a child under 21. 3. Determine whether the other individuals who are applying are eligible based on their income as follows: - Compute income eligibility according to the rules for the coverage group. - Do not include individuals who receive HH or are eligible but not yet approved for HH, in the income and resource eligibility determination of the other individuals who are applying for NMP or MNO . Exception: For the remaining individuals in the household who are applying for or receiving HB, the individual who receives HH or is eligible for but not yet approved for HH, may be or must be included in the eligibility determination for HB. The inclusion depends on the individualâs relationship to the other household members.

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: For a household of Healthy Beginnings (HB) applicants/recipients and individuals who are applying for or receiving other MA coverage, the CAO will do the following: 1. Determine whether the HB applicant/recipient and other family members who are included in the income count are eligible based on income. (See Chapter 312, Affordable Care Act/Modified Adjusted Gross Income - MAGI ) . 2. Determine whether the other people who are applying for or receiving MA are eligible based on income. - Compute income eligibility according to the income-eligibility requirements for the right coverage group of MA. - Pregnant women and qualified HB children related by blood or marriage must be included in the same income-eligibility group for HB. - An individual who (a) has HB or is eligible but not yet approved for HB and (b) is a legally responsible relative (LRR) spouse or parent) to someone applying for another NMP or MNO coverage group of MA must be included in the income-eligibility determination for the applicant/recipient group. The individual to whom the HB individual is an LRR must also be included. - Children who receive HB or are eligible but not yet approved for HB, may be included in the income eligibility of the applicant/recipient groups for NMP and MNO, including their parentsâ groups. - The income of the HB applicant/recipient who is included with the other individuals in the household applying for or receiving MA is treated according to the rules in Chapter 360, NMP Deductions and Chapter 361, MNO Deductions . - If an HB applicant/recipient is included in the income-eligibility determination for an applicant/recipient group for MA, the income limit is increased by the number of HB applicants/recipients, including any unborn child. NOTE: The HB applicant who also meets the eligibility requirements for the HH program is included in the eligibility determination for the remaining members in the household applying for or receiving MA, according to whichever is most beneficial to all members. The applicant/recipient who meets eligibility requirements for both HB and HH must choose one coverage group when determining eligibility for other family members.

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: If the applicant/recipient chooses HH, the HH individual is not included in the eligibility determination for the other members. If the applicant/recipient chooses HB, the HB individual is treated according to the requirements of the program that the other family members are applying for or receiving. For a household of NMP applicants/recipients and individuals who are applying for or receiving other MA coverage, the CAO will do the following: Reminder: Eligibility for NMP for the Family (NMPF) as a single budget group must be considered before determining eligibility for any one individual. 1. Determine MA eligibility for the individual or couple applying for or receiving NMP based on income. (See Chapter 368, Determining Eligibility for NMP .)

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: NOTE: Do not include individuals receiving HH or who are eligible but not yet approved for HH, and do not deem their income. NOTE: There will never be an SSI -related MNO spouse with an SSI-related NMP spouse. If both spouses are disabled , they must apply as an SSI-related couple for HH first and then for NMP. NOTE: A spouse or parent can be SSI-related NMP while the other family members are TANF -related or GA-related. A spouse or parent receiving HB or eligible but not yet approved for HB, must be included in the income eligibility decision for the spouse or child applying for or receiving SSI-related NMP. A child receiving HB or eligible but not yet approved for HB, may be included in the income eligibility determination for the parent applying for or receiving NMP. 2. If the spouse/parent is a noneligible legally responsible relative (LRR) or noneligible specified relative , deem the income and resources according to Chapter 355, Deeming Income and Resources . 3. Determine if the other individuals who are applying for or receiving MA are income eligible as follows: - Compute income eligibility according to the income-eligibility requirements for the right coverage group. - Individuals who (a) are receiving SSI related NMP or eligible but not yet approved for SSI-related NMP, and who (b) are legally responsible relatives (such as a spouse or parent) to others applying for MNO or TANF-related or GA-related NMP, are not included in the income and resource eligibility determination for the other individuals applying for MNO, TANF-related NMP, or GA-related NMP. Exception: Someone who is approved for, or eligible but not yet approved for NMP, may or must affect the eligibility determination for individuals who are applying for or receiving HB or HH, depending on the individualâs relationship to the household members.

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: For a household of MNO applicants/recipients and family members who are applying for or receiving other MA coverage, the CAO will do the following: 1. Determine income eligibility of the individuals who are applying for or with SSI-related, TANF-related, or GA-related MNO and are related by blood or marriage as if they are one applicant/recipient group, according to Chapter 369, Determining Eligibility for MNO . NOTE: Do not include individuals receiving HH, or eligible but not yet approved for HH, as family members, and do not deem their income NOTE: All immediate family members who are applying for or receiving MNO must be treated as if they are one applicant/recipient group. The only exception to this is step relationships. - Individuals who (a) receive HB, or are eligible but not yet approved for HB, and who (b) are legally responsible relatives (such as a spouse or parent) to someone who is applying for MNO must be included in the income eligibility determination. - Children who receive HB or are eligible but not yet approved for HB, may be included in the parentâs income eligibility determination for MNO. - The countable income of someone receiving HB who is included with the other family members applying for or receiving MA is treated according to Chapter 361, MNO Deductions and Chapter 369, Determining on Eligibility for MNO . - If someone who receives HB is included in the income-eligibility decision for an applicant/recipient group for MNO, the income limit is increased, depending on how many HB people are included. Unborn children are also included. The CAO will not count the resources of individuals in an applicant/recipient group if the individual is: - Living in the household with a spouse under age 21. - Living in the household with an adopted or natural child under age 21.

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: - Living in the household with a stepchild under age 21. - Living with, taking care of, and in charge of a child under age 21. - Deem income and resources of the nonapplicant/nonrecipient LRR according to Chapter 355, Deeming Income and Resources . NOTE: When deeming the income of an individual who is an LRR to both SSI-related and TANF-related or GA-related MNO applicants/recipients in the same group, the CAO treats the nonapplicant member as an SSI-related LRR. There is no deduction given for a dependent of the LRR who is included in the applicant/recipient group for MNO. If the LRR's countable income after allowable deductions is more than 50 percent of the federal benefit rate, the LRR is treated as an SSI-related applicant/recipient. The highest income allowed for the applicant/recipient group for MNO is increased by one individual to include the LRR. The LRR is not approved for MNO. NOTE: When deeming the income of an NA member who is an LRR to both TANF-related and GA-related individuals in the same applicant/recipient group, the CAO treats the NA member as a TANF-related LRR. Example: A household is made up of a husband, his wife, their two children, and his child from a previous marriage. The wife and their two children are applying for MNO. The CAO determines one deduction for basic living needs. Monthly Medically Needy Income Limit (MNIL) $675 - 5 individuals (3 applicants plus the husband and his child) [MNO monthly income limit for 5 individuals] -467 - 3 applicants [MNO monthly income limit for 3 individuals] - If the rule about not including resources for individuals under age 21 does not apply to an individual in the applicant/recipient group for MNO, the CAO determines resources according to Chapter 340, Resources .

title: 310.5 Determining Eligibility for Multiple Applicant/Recipient Groups
text: - If the resources for individuals whose resources cannot be excluded are over the limit, the whole applicant/recipient group is ineligible for MNO.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: Minimum Essential Coverage (MEC) Reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and Individuals, OPS161201 (Published December 6, 2016) Medical Assistance Program Changes due to the Introduction of Enhanced Federal Medical Assistance Percentage, OPS160305 (Published March 10, 2016) Effective January 1, 2014, the Affordable Care Act (ACA) changed Medical Assistance (MA) rules in Pennsylvania. Modified Adjusted Gross Income (MAGI) is the income methodology used for determining MA eligibility for children through age 18, parents (biological, adoptive or step), caretakers, pregnant women and adults ages 19-64. M A rules have not changed for individuals whose eligibility is determined on the basis other than MAGI: - Individuals for whom income determinations are not required, such as individuals receiving Supplemental Security Income (SSI), federal foster care or adoption assistance recipients or recipients of Breast and Cervical Cancer Prevention and Treatment (BCCPT) services. - Individuals whose eligibility is based on being 65 or older, disabled or blind. - Individuals evaluated for Medically Needy Only (MNO). - Individuals evaluated for Long Term Care (LTC) facility services or Home and Community Based Services (HCBS). - Individuals eligible for Medicare cost sharing. - Individuals evaluated for General Assistance (GA) related MA. The MA category MG is used to identify the majority of MAGI-related budget groups, (See Chapter 305, Appendix E: Medical Assistance Cascade Table). Individuals receiving category MG will be assigned a program status code based on the coverage group. MAGI categories and program status codes: - MG00 - Pregnant and postpartum women and children (under age 1) with income at or below 215 percent of the Federal Poverty Income Guideline (FPIG), children (ages 1-5) with income at or below 157 percent of the FPIG, children (ages 6-18) with income at or below 100 percent of the FPIG , and children (ages 6-18) with income between 100 and 133 percent of the FPIG with Minimum Essential Coverage (MEC) health insurance (See Chapter 312.14 Children Ages 6-18)

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: - MG18 - Pregnant and postpartum women and children under 1 who are eligible for extended medical coverage despite an increase in income over 215 percent of the FPIG. Children under 19 enrolled in certain MA or SSI categories who become ineligible due to excess income, whereabouts unknown, failure to provide information, or failure to keep an appointment during their 12-month continuous eligibility period. - MG19 - Children age 6-18 with income between 100 percent and 133 percent of the FPIG without Minimum Essential Coverage (MEC) health insurance (See Chapter 312.14 Children Ages 6-18). - MG27 - Individuals with income at or below 33 percent of the FPIG. Individuals could be parents/caretakers and children ages 0-17, or children age 18, if they are a full-time secondary or vocational student. - MG23 - Individuals eligible for Transitional Medical Assistance (TMA) due to an increase in alimony/spousal support. Individuals with alimony that is not countable for MAGI MA are NOT eligible for Transitional Medical Assistance (TMA) in the MG 23 category. - MG38 - Individuals ages 21-64 who are temporarily disabled and incarcerated in a State Correctional Institution. See Chapter 392.2 for more information. - MG39 - Individuals ages 21-64 who are temporarily disabled and incarcerated in a county prison. See Chapter 392.2 for more information. - MG71 - Individuals eligible for Transitional Medical Assistance (TMA) due to an increase in earned income. - MG90 - Individuals ages 19-64 with a permanent disability (MRT or SSA verified) with income at or below 102 percent of the FPIG and are not eligible for Medicare or individuals ages 19-20 with income at or below the Enhanced Medically Needy Only (MNO) limit and are not eligible for Medicare. (See Appendix A for Enhanced MNO income limits). If an individual has refused or withdrawn from Medicare, they should still be considered as entitled and receiving Medicare. Individuals who report a pregnancy while receiving MG 90 will remain in MG 90 until the end of their postpartum period or next renewal, whichever comes first.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: - MG91 - Indiv iduals ages 19-64, including individuals with a permanent disability that is not verified by SSA or MRT, with income at or below 133 percent of the FPIG and are not eligible for Medicare. If an individual has refused or withdrawn from Medicare, they should still be considered as entitled and receiving Medicare. Individuals who report a pregnancy while receiving MG 91 will remain in MG 91 until the end of their postpartum period or next renewal, whichever comes first. - MG99 - Individuals who are suspended from MA for less than 2 years due to incarceration. - PC40 - Former foster care recipients. - PSF15 - Individuals with income at or below 215 percent of the FPIG who only receive Family Planning Services (FPS) and were first evaluated for MA. - PSF14 - Individuals with income at or below 215 percent of the FPIG who only receive FPS and were not reviewed for MA because they claimed "good cause" or were under 21 and only wanted to be reviewed for FPS or are under 21 and are over income for PSF15 using MAGI net income. MAGI-related budget groups are built using the following target types: - P - Pregnant Women (MG00, MG18, MG90, MG91). - E â Pregnant Women â Extended Postpartum (MG00). - I - Infant less than 1 year old (MG27, MG00, MG23, MG71, MG17). - N - Infant less than 1 year old (MG18) or individual age 19-20 (MG90). - C - Children less than 19 years old (MG18) or children age 1 -5 (MG27, MG00, MG23, MG71, MG17). - Y - Children age 6 to 18 (MG27, MG19, MG00, MG23, MG71, MG17). - T - Parent/Caretaker (MG27, MG23, MG71, MG17). - D - Permanently disabled, MRT or SSA verified (MG90). NOTE: MG91 (except MG91P), PC40, PSF15 and PSF14 are built with no target types.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: The CAO must decide who can get MA using the rules in this chapter and the following chapters: Medical Assistance Eligibility Handbooks - Chapter 304 Application - Chapter 350 Income NOTE: The individual must report changes in gross monthly earned income of more than $100 and of gross monthly unearned income of more than $50. All other changes, including new sources of income, must continue to be reported. - Chapter 378 Verification - Chapter 379 Reconsidering Denied Application and Reopening Closed Benefits - Supplemental Handbook Chapter 870 Appeal and Fair Hearing - Supplemental Handbook Chapter 950 Enumeration

title: 312.1 General Policy
section_title: 312.12 Pregnant Women and Children under Age One
text: A pregnant woman or a qualified child up to age 1 can get MAGI-related MA if household income is equal to or less than 215 percent of the Federal Poverty Income Guideline (FPIG ) for the household size (See Appendix A ) . The pregnant woman, once approved, continues to be eligible for MA coverage through the end of the 12 th month when the12-month postpartum period ends, regardless of her income or a change in circumstances . ( See Chapter 338.42 ) The newborn whose mother was getting MA or CHIP at the time of birth can get MA coverage up to age one, regardless of the parentsâ income, per the Children's Health Insurance Program Reauthorization Act of 2009 (CHIPRA). Children under one who were not born to a mother on MA or CHIP at the time of birth are eligible for a 12-month continuous eligibility period (See Section 312.131 ). NOTE: If the CAO did not know about the pregnancy and learns that MA or SSI benefits were closed during the pregnancy, it must approve MA coverage from the date of the closing through the last day of the month in which the 12-month postpartum period ends. Newborns and pregnant/postpartum individuals can only be closed during their periods of continuous eligibility for the following reasons: - Permanent Move Out of State - If the CAO determines that the opening of benefits was incorrect due to fraud or abuse or agency error. When a provider or other outside source asks for MA benefits for a child under age one, the CAO must review the familyâs case record to see if MA , SSI, or CHIP benefits were open for the mother at any time during the pregnancy. If so, the CAO must authorize MA benefits for the child from the date of birth until the first birthday.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: A qualified child age 1-5 can receive MAGI-related MA if household income is equal to or less than 157 percent of the FPIG for the household size (See Appendix A ). A qualified child age 6-18 can receive MAGI-related MA if household income is equal to or less than 133 percent of the FPIG for the household size (See Appendix A ). If a child age 6-18 has income under 100 percent of the FPIG for the household size or a child (ages 6-18) with income between 100 and 133 percent of the FPIG with Minimum Essential Coverage (MEC) health insurance, MG00 is assigned.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: If the child age 6-18 has income between 100 percent and 133 percent of the FPIG without Minimum Essential Coverage (MEC) health insurance, MG19 is assigned. Health insurance may be reported and available to the CAO on applications, renewals, through individual reported changes, or Third-Party Liability (TPL) screens in the case record, etc. If the reported health insurance is considered MEC (see MEC Desk Guide for information about what insurance is MEC), the CAO will check the âMinimum Essential Coverage?â box on the Individual Attributes screen when information is available that shows a child, age 18 and under, has MEC health insurance. It is important to check this box for all applying children age 18 and under due to changes to category of eligibility that may occur. If health insurance coverage is reported on a new application, on a renewal, or through individual reported changes, the CAO will update the MEC indicator on the Individual Attributes screen accordingly. The CAO will not require that the insurance question be answered on the application. The CAO will follow the existing verification policy in Chapter 378.3 to verify health insurance. NOTE: MA should not be delayed or terminated for an individual whose responsible relative fails to cooperate in identifying and verifying TPL information. Health insurance may also be systematically updated by TPL batch processes. Any time the CAO takes action on a case with an open MG 19 category, the CAO will review the TPL screens and ensure that the âMinimum Essential Coverage?â box is checked if the TPL screens list MEC insurance. Children age 6-18 with household income between 100 and 133 percent FPL with any type of health insurance reported on the application are authorized in the MG 00 category. The reported health insurance and the MEC check box will not be populated in eCIS. The next time the CAO takes action on the case they must update the MEC indicator on the Individual Attributes screen and review TPL information.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: For a child age 18, student status will impact the program status code that is assigned. An 18-year old can be eligible as MG27 if they meet all eligibility criteria and they have the following student status: If the 18-year old does not have the above student status, MG00 or MG19 can be assigned if all other eligibility criteria are met. 312.141 Continuous Eligibility for Children under Age 19 Children under age 19 eligible for MA or a n SSI budget at application or renewal remain eligible for MA coverage in MG 18 C until their next 12-month renewal or until the child reaches age 19, regardless of changes that affect their MA eligibility. Children in certain MA categories are not eligible for continuous eligibility ( See Appendix H ). CAOs must not change the MA renewal date for budgets that qualify for continuous eligibility while aligning other budgets. The CAO must close children under 19 if: - The child turns age 19. AGE 319 alert will continue to be received to review eligibility for a child turning age 19. The AGE 053 alert created for CE under age four will be decommissioned. - The child is not eligible for MA at their renewal. NOTE: If a child under 19 is ineligible for MA at renewal, the CAO must close the child in Reapplication mode during or after the renewal month. The CAO should review the case to ensure that the child has received a full 12 months of MA coverage prior to closing the childâs MA budget. The child will be reviewed for CHIP or referred to Pennie (Pennsylvaniaâs Health Insurance Marketplace) in accordance with existing rules if the child is ineligible for MA at renewal.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: - The child moves out of state. - A voluntary withdrawal request is received for MA. - The CAO determines that the childâs MA was opened in error. - Citizenship or satisfactory immigration status is not verified following a Reasonable Opportunity Period (ROP) The CAO must not close children under 19 for: - Exceeding income limits. - Failure to keep an appointment. When a CAO processes an inter-county transfer for a child who was open for an MA category eligible for Continuous Eligibility and the child is determined ineligible for MA in a new county due to a change in circumstances, the CAO will open the child in MG 18 C with the original renewal date.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: A parent (biological, adoptive or step) or caretaker can receive MAGI-related MA. The following criteria must be met: - Have care and control of a child age 0-17 or age 18 and a full-time secondary or vocational/ technical school (vo-tech) student. - Have household income equal to or less than 33 percent of the FPIG (See Appendix A ). NOTE: Individuals other than parents with care and control of a child in the MAGI-related household can be caretakers, if the parent is not living in the household. For MAGI-related MA, an individual does not need to be related to the child to be a specified relative in the parent/caretaker category. Caretakers are individuals age 19 or older. NOTE: If no parents are taking care and control of the child in a household, only one non-parent caretaker is a valid target. If multiple non-parent caretakers are present in the household, only one caretaker will be selected in order of age, oldest to youngest. To ensure the correct MA category is opened for parents/caretakers and unemancipated children age 18 and under, the care and control indicators must be marked on the Relationship screen if the individual is responsible for care and control of a child age 18 or under.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: Individuals age 19-64 can receive MAGI-related MA if household income is equal to or less than 133 percent of the FPIG for the household size ( See Appendix A ) . If an individual age 19-20 has income at or below the Enhanced MNO limit (See Appendix A ), the individual will be assigned category/program status code/target type of MG90N. If an individual 19-64 has a permanent disability (SSA or MRT certified), has income at or below 102 percent of the FPIG, (See Appendix A ) and is not entitled to Medicare, the individual will be assigned category/program status code/target type of MG90D. All other individuals 19-64 will be assigned category/program status code MG91. If a woman between the ages of 19-64 is receiving MG90 or MG91 and reports a pregnancy, she will be opened in MG90P or MG91P respectively, and she will remain in MG90P or MG91P until the end of her postpartum period. Individuals who report a disability will be evaluated for non-MAGI MA and MAGI MA. If the individualâs disability is not verified by SSA or MRT, the CAO will review for MAGI eligibility first. The CAO will review for non-MAGI MA if the individual is not eligible for MAGI MA, or if the individual is determined disabled by SSA or MRT. If the individualâs disability is verified by SSA or MRT, the CAO will review for non-MAGI MA prior to authorizing MG90D or MG91. NOTE : For MG90 and MG91, the individual must not be eligible for or receiving Medicare.

title: 312.1 General Policy
text: See Chapter 338, Medical Assistance Benefits for information about MA benefits. The CAO does not consider resources when deciding an individual's eligibility in a MAGI-related MA category.

title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
section_title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
text: MAGI-related MA household composition is determined based on federal tax rules with some exceptions. When the CAO determines eligibility for MAGI-related MA, the CAO must first determine tax filing status and tax relationships between the individuals in the household. If the individual does not answer tax questions on the application or renewal form, the tax relationships are considered unknown. The CAO will use non-filer rules to determine eligibility for MAGI MA. NOTE: If the CAO completes an interview for other benefits at application or renewal, the caseworker will determine the tax filing relationships during the interview if they are not listed on the application or renewal form. Individuals who receive MA in either MAGI- related categories or non-MAGI related categories are included in the MAGI determinations of other members of the household if they have a tax relationship to the individual being evaluated for MAGI - related MA. NOTE: When determining eligibility for a household which includes a pregnant woman, the pregnant woman is counted as herself plus the number of children she is expected to deliver.

title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
text: Each applicantâs eligibility is based on one of three tax filing statuses: - Tax Filer - an individual who plans to file taxes for the current tax year. NOTE: When spouses file a joint tax return, they are both considered a Tax Filer. - Tax Dependent - an individual who is claimed as a tax dependent by someone who plans to file taxes. NOTE: An individual who is both a tax dependent and a tax filer will be considered a tax dependent. NOTE: The terms Tax Filer and Tax Dependent always refer to the expected status for the current coverage year, and not to any prior filing status. - Non-Filer - an individual who is not a tax filer and not a tax dependent. Individuals who do not live in the applicant's household but have a tax relationship with anyone in the household are recognized as an Outside of the Household (OOTH) individual. An OOTH is included when determining a MAGI household if: - has a tax-relationship to an applicant - does not live in the household - cannot be an applying member of the household - cannot be a payment name of the case Though an OOTH is considered part of the individual's household for MAGI purposes, the OOTH and their income may not always count toward the MAGI-related determination. See Section 312.22, MAGI Filer/Non-Filer rules for Household Composition.

title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
section_title: 312.22 Â MAGI Filer/Non-Filer Rules for Household Composition
text: When determining eligibility for individuals who are tax filers and tax dependents, MAGI Rules for household composition are as follows. Tax Filer Rules for Household Composition The household composition of a tax filer is: - All of the tax filerâs claimed dependents, and - The tax filerâs spouse, if living with the tax filer. NOTE: Married couples living together will be included in the household of the other spouse regardless of whether they expect to file a joint tax return. Exceptions to Tax Filer Rules: - If the information the tax filer provides is questionable based on tax dependent criteria in Appendix D and the filer cannot provide proof of their tax dependents, then the household is determined by non-filer rules. Example: Anne (age 49) states on her application that she will claim her daughter, Susan (age 28), and her son, Ian (age 15), on her tax return. Susan is employed and earns $1,000 per month. Because Susanâs income exceeds the income criteria for a qualifying relative being claimed as a tax dependent , the CAO can request that Anne provide proof that she claims Susan as a tax dependent. If Anne does not provide proof, the CAO will use non-filer rules to determine Anneâs MAGI household. Tax Dependent Rules for Household Composition The household composition of a tax dependent is: - The spouse of the tax dependent's tax filer, if living with the tax filer. - All other tax dependents of the tax filer. - The tax dependentâs spouse, if living with the individual. NOTE: Spouses cannot be tax dependents of each other. Exceptions to Tax Dependent Rules: - If the individual is claimed as a tax dependent by someone other than a spouse or a parent (biological, adoptive or step), the individualâs household composition is determined by non-filer rules.

title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
section_title: 312.22 Â MAGI Filer/Non-Filer Rules for Household Composition
text: Example: Emily and Jacob have legal custody of their grandson, Andrew (age 8). Emily and Jacob claim Andrew as their Tax Dependent. Andrewâs MAGI household composition will use Non-Filer rules and will include Andrew only. - If a child under age 19 lives with both parents, but both parents do not expect to file taxes jointly and only one parent claims the child as a tax dependent, the childâs household composition is determined by non-filer rules. 42 CFR § 435.603.(f)(2)(ii) Example: Maria and Jorge are not married but are living together and are the parents of Brian (age 4). Maria and Jorge will not file taxes jointly and Maria will claim Brian as a Tax Dependent. Brianâs MAGI household composition will use Non-Filer rules and will include Brian, Maria and Jorge. NOTE: This exception does not apply when the child lives with more than two parents and is claimed as a tax dependent. In this situation, the CAO will follow tax filer rules to determine the childâs household composition. Example: Bob and Denise are married and living together. Doug is also living in the same household and shares a child with Denise. Allen, age 4, is Dougâs and Deniseâs common child. Bob and Denise are filing taxes jointly and claiming Allen as a tax dependent. Allenâs MAGI household composition will use tax filer rules and will include Allen, Bob, and Denise. - If a child under age 19 is claimed as a tax dependent by non-custodial parents, the childâs household composition is determined by non-filer rules. 42 CFR § 435.603(f)(2)(iii) Example: James and Caitlin are divorced and do not live together. Caitlin has primary physical custody of their son, Rob (age 10) James claims Rob as a tax dependent. Robâs MAGI household composition will use Non-Filer rules and will include Rob and Caitlin. Though James is an OOTH, he will not be counted in the MAGI household since the Non-Filer rules will be used.

title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
section_title: 312.22 Â MAGI Filer/Non-Filer Rules for Household Composition
text: - If a child under age 19 is claimed as a tax dependent by their parents who are married and who will file taxes jointly but one parent lives outside of the household due to separation or pending divorce, the childâs household composition is determined by non-filer rules. T he parent living outside of the household will not be counted in the budget. Example: Frank and Laura are married and will file taxes jointly, claiming their son, Derrick (age 9). Frank and Laura are separated, pending a divorce, and Frank is living outside of the household. Derrickâs MAGI household will be Laura and Derrick only. Though Frank is an OOTH, he is not included in the MAGI household since Non-Filer rules will be used. Example : Steve and Meg are married and will file taxes jointly, claiming their children Hailey and Stan (ages 3 and 6). Steve works for a trucking company and lives out of town Monday through Friday each week. Steve's permanent address is his residence with his family and he returns home each weekend. Steve is not an OOTH. Steve is counted in the MAGI household, and tax filer/tax dependent rules are used. Non-Filer Rules for Household Composition When determining eligibility for individuals who are not tax filers and not tax dependents, the Non-Filer Rules for household composition are followed. The household composition of an adult (age 19 and above) non-filer is: - The non-filerâs spouse who lives in the household. - The non-filerâs biological, adoptive or step-children under the age of 19 who live in the household. If a child (under age 19) is a target whose eligibility is being determined under non-filer rules, the childâs household composition includes:

title: 312.2 Who is in the MAGI Household?
section_title: 312.22 Â MAGI Filer/Non-Filer Rules for Household Composition
text: - The childâs biological, adoptive or step parents who live in the household. - The childâs biological, adoptive, half or step siblings under the age of 19 who live in the household. - The child's spouse if living in the household. - The child's biological, adoptive or stepchildren who live in the household. 42 CFR § 435.603(f)(3)(ii) 42 CFR § 435.603(f)(3)(iii) - If a child under age 19 is considered a non-filer and is also considered emancipated, the child's parents and siblings under the age of 19 (if they live with the child) will not be included in the child's determination.

title: 312.3 Period of Eligibility
text: Medical Assistance (MA) Fast Track Enrollment Enhancements, OPS170805 (Published August 30, 2017) - The date when the CAO gets and stamps a signed application form or MA Fast Track consent form ( PA 1945 ) . - The earliest date the application is signed by the applicant if it is submitted by an MA provider or at any site chosen by the Department of Human Services (DHS). - The date a COMPASS application or MA Fast Track consent form is submitted online. - The date of first treatment or admission if the COMPASS application is submitted by an MA provider. - The date the application was submitted to the State-Based Exchange (SBE) if the application is forwarded to the CAO from the SBE . Eligibility ends on the date the individual no longer meets the eligibility requirements. A child born to a woman who is receiving MA or CHIP on the date of the child's birth automatically receives MA coverage for one year. (See Section 309.2, Newborn Eligibility ) .

title: 312.3 Period of Eligibility
text: An individual can apply for benefits for medical services in the past if he or she has an unpaid medical expense from before the application date. Benefits for past medical services may be approved for the period starting with the first day of the third month before the date of application. The individual may choose to apply for either (a) retroactive-only, (b) continuing-only, or (c) combined retroactive and continuing MA benefits. NOTE: When processing a combined retroactive and ongoing MA application, the CAO should not delay processing ongoing MA while waiting for verification that is needed for retroactive MA. NOTE: There is no spend-down for MAGI MA determinations.

title: 312.4 Income
text: When deciding on an individualâs eligibility in a MAGI MA -related category, the CAO must count the monthly earned income and unearned income of every individual included in the individualâs tax household. MA financial eligibility is based on current monthly household income in most scenarios. For MAGI MA, some determinations must be reviewed for eligibility based on an annualized calculation. (See Section 312. 4 3, Annualized Income ) Counted and excluded income for MAGI is based on what is included in Adjusted Gross Income (AGI) when filing a tax return with the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). The following income types are not typically included in AGI, but are considered countable for MA purposes: - Retirement, Survivors, and Disability Insurance (RSDI). NOTE: To determine if RSDI is counted for a child or tax dependent see ( Section 312. 4 4, Income of Children and Tax Dependents ). - Foreign earned income (US citizens living abroad). Certain excluded types and sources of income for MAGI-related MA are listed in this chapter. They are not counted in the MA financial eligibility decision.

title: 312.4 Income
text: The CAO must count payments for services as earned income. Earned income includes, but is not limited to the following: NOTE: Because MAGI uses IRS guidelines for determining household income, all expenses reported on the household's 1040 Schedule C (Profit or Loss from Business) are allowable deductions for MAGI. This includes offsetting self-employment losses against other income reported on the IRS form 1040 (see Section 312. 5 3, Offsetting Self-Employment Losses) . - Lump Sum-earnings (only in the month it is received). The CAO must get proof of all earned income and include the proof and the amount in the case record in accordance with Section 312. 6 1, Verification Requirements .

title: 312.4 Income
text: The CAO must look at the total amount of unearned income in the household when determining MAGI-related MA eligibility. Unearned income includes, but is not limited to, the following: - Retirement benefits, private pensions and annuities. - Retirement, Survivors, and Disability Insurance (RSDI) benefits. NOTE: RSDI income of a child or tax dependent may or may not count for MAGI-related MA. (See Section 312. 4 4, Income of Children and Tax Dependents ) . NOTE: Non-taxable sick benefits are excluded from income. (See Section 312.45 Excluded Income ). - Dividends , royalties and interest posted or received in the calendar month, including tax exempt interest. - Alimony payments for settlements prior to 01/01/2019. - Childâs unearned income that includes interest and dividends. - Net capital gains (profit after subtracting capital losses). - Rental income when the property is managed by a third party. NOTE : If the rental property is not managed by third party consider the income self-employment. NOTE: A prize is a cash payment won in a contest, lottery, or game relating to luck. An individual usually gets an award because of a decision by a court, board of arbitration, or similar legal body. An individual may receive a prize as a lump sum or in regular payments. 55 Pa. Code § 140.61 - Lump Sum- unearned (only in the month it is received). - Cash support can only be counted if the person providing the cash support is claiming the individual as a tax dependent, and the tax dependent is not their spouse or their child (biological, adopted or step). If a non-responsible relative is not claiming the individual as a tax dependent, the cash support would not count in the individualâs eligibility determination. Example 1: An uncle gives $200 every month to their 25-year-old nephew. The uncle does not claim the nephew as a tax dependent. The uncle does not expect their nephew to repay this money. The $200 is not counted in the nephew's monthly unearned income.

title: 312.4 Income
text: Example 2: A parent gives $200 every month to their 19-year-old child , who the parent claims as a tax dependent. The parent does not expect their child to repay this money. The $200 is not counted in the child âs unearned income. Example 3: A parent gives $200 every month to her 19-year-old child , who the parent does not claim as a tax dependent. The parent does not expect their child to repay this money. The $200 is not counted in the child âs unearned income. Example 1: A n individual gives $200 every month to their elderly parent , who the individual claims as a tax dependent. The individual does not expect their parent to repay this money. Count the full amount after the first $50. The parent 's countable cash support is $150 per month. Example 2: A grand parent gives $200 every month to their 19-year-old grand child , who the grand parent claims as a tax dependent. The grand parent does not expect their grand child to repay this money. Count the full amount after the first $50. The grand child 's countable cash support is $150 per month. NOTE: Cash support is money received on a frequent and regular basis with no expectation of repayment. NOTE: Child support is not considered cash support. The CAO must verify all unearned income and include the proof and the amount in the case record in accordance with Section 312. 6 1, Verification Requirements

title: 312.4 Income
text: If a MAGI budget group's monthly income (earned and/or unearned) exceeds the monthly income limit and the income is expected to decrease or terminate, an annualized income calculation must be performed. The expected decrease or termination must be verified in accordance with Section 312. 6 1 Verification Requirements and Section 312. 6 2 Reasonable Compatibility . To determine annual household income, total the household's countable earned and unearned income from January 1 through December 31 of the year in which the eligibility is being determined and compare to the annual income limit. NOTE: The caseworker must manually calculate the annual income. If the income is within the limit, data enter the earned and unearned income respectively with a frequency code of â9-Annually.â The caseworker may need to create two income screens or complete a manual override if income annualization is not allowed for other budgets or programs on the case record.
