The system will automatically mail the L/IN. - If the household submits a completed SAR form or L/IN before the extended due date with required verification, determine continued eligibility and the benefit amount using the information on the form, and determine a new SAR period. - If the information is not received by the extended due date, determine good cause and record the decision in the case record. Reinstate the case if good cause is determined. - Mail a Confirming Notice to tell the household of any action taken, such as suspension, termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the SAR form.

title: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms
section_title: 571.44 Incomplete SAR Form Received after Due Date
text: When a SAR form is received after the SAR form due date but by the last day to track the SAR form and not all questions are answered or no verification is attached, the CAO will: - Track the SAR form as incomplete, if time permits. NOTE: The CFSAR system suspends benefits when the CAO either tracks the SAR form as incomplete or does not track the SAR form because the household did not turn in a completed form in time for the CAO to track it. The system will automatically send an L/IN to the household for completion. - Determine continued eligibility and benefit amount using the information provided if the household turns in either a complete SAR form or a complete L/IN by the extended due date, and establish a new SAR period. - Promptly mail a confirming notice to tell the household of any action taken, such as suspension, termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the SAR form. Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008

title: 571_Appendix B
text: When the SAR form is not returned or is returned late or incomplete, the Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN, PA 564-A ) is sent. (See Chapter 571, Appendix A .) The L/IN serves as the Advance Notice to close for the cash and SNAP programs. The L/IN includes: - Questions from the SAR that were not answered, along with the pre-populated information as it appeared on the SAR form. - The extended due date when the L/IN must be in the CAO . NOTE: The L/IN does not serve as an Advance Notice for the Medical Assistance program. To close or change a Medical Assistance program through the SAR process, the CAO must send an Advance Notice. See Medical Assistance Handbook, Section 377.4 . Question #1 will always be printed and answered on the L/IN for use as a reference for the recipient to answer other questions. If this question was not answered or completed by the recipient, the question âDid anyone move into or out of your household ?â will also appear on the L/IN. The completion of the tracking screen based on the information provided on the SAR form will directly impact the information on the L/IN. For example: - If all questions are incomplete, and the ALL boxes on the SAR form and on the CCMRTK screen are checked, then all questions and data from the SAR form will be printed on the L/IN. - If the SAR form is not signed, and the UNSIGNED boxes on the SAR form and on the CCMRTK screen are checked, then the L/IN will tell the client, âYou did not sign your semiannual reporting form. Please sign the certification section and return the form.â 1. Did anyone move into or out of your household? If yes, list who and their relationship to you. Question #1 asks for a report of changes in who lives in the household. Block 1 is checked if question #1 is not answered or is incomplete. There is no longer a requirement to track verification for this question. The caseworker should contact the recipient as needed.

title: 571_Appendix B
text: 2. Did any household member start a new job, change a job, or stop working? If yes, list any changes, such as job start date, end date, date of first pay, how often paid). Provide proof (pay stubs, employer statements, etc.). Question #2 asks for a report of employment changes. Block 2 is checked if question #2 is not answered and both the question and the data are printed on the L/IN. If the household reports changes, verification must be provided. Block 2V is checked if proof of changes is inadequate or not attached, and the statement âYou did not provide proof of any changesâ is printed. If both Block 2 and 2V are checked, the question and the data will be printed on the L/IN. 3. Provide proof (pay stubs, employer statements, etc.) of all work income any household member received in the month of [reporting month] Question #3 asks for a report of earned income. If a household member has earned income in the reporting month, proof must be provided. If all proof is not provided or is inadequate, Block 3V is checked, and the following statement is printed on the L/IN: âYou did not provide proof of any changes." 4. Did any household member lose or start receiving income or have a change in amount? If yes, list any changes. Provide proof (award letter, support court orders, etc.). Question #4 asks for a report of unearned income changes. Block 4 is checked if question #4 is not answered and both the question and the data are printed on the L/IN. If the recipient reports changes, verification must be provided. Block 4V is checked if proof of changes is inadequate or not attached, and the statement "You did not provide proof of any changes" is printed. If both Block 4 and 4V are checked, the question and the data will be printed on the L/IN.

title: 571_Appendix B
text: 5. Is the address on this form your current address? If no, what is your new address? Provide proof (Examples: lease, landlord statement, deed, etc.). If you receive SNAP benefits and you have moved, what are your shelter (rent/mortgage) and utility costs? Do you pay for your own heating and/or air conditioning? Question #5 asks for a report of an address change. Block 5 is checked if the question is not answered, and Question #5 is printed on the L/IN. Data is not filled in for this question, but the recipient has space to provide a new address. The portion of the question regarding shelter and utility costs does not need to be answered or verified and will not be tracked. Recipients are advised in the Instructions section that if this information is not provided, it may result in less or no SNAP benefits. 6. Did any household member have a change in the amount he is requested to pay? If yes, list any changes. Provide copy of support court order or letter and proof of payment. Question #6 asks for a report of the amount of support paid for children outside the household. This question is not required to be tracked and has no corresponding block on the SAR form. Recipients are advised in the instructions that changes in support paid will affect SNAP benefits and that they may receive less or no SNAP benefits if this information is not provided and verified. This question will appear on the L/IN only if the SAR form is not received or if the ALL box is checked on the SAR form. 7. Are there any changes? If yes, list any changes. Provide copy of bill or statement from caregiver. Question #7 asks for a report of the amount paid for child care or for care of a sick or disabled person. Recipients are advised in the instructions that changes in support paid will affect SNAP benefits and that they may receive less or no SNAP benefits if this information is not provided and verified. This question will appear on the L/IN only if the SAR form is not received or if the ALL box is checked on the SAR form.

title: 571_Appendix B
text: 8. Has the information in this section changed? Does any household member have resources not listed above? If you answered yes to either question, list any changes. Provide proof (copy of bank statement, vehicle registration, etc.) Question #8 asks for a report of household resources. Block 8 is checked if the recipient does not answer either or both questions. Exception: The CAO must not mark this question as incomplete, regardless of the answers, if resource information is not required in the eligibility determination according to Medical Assistance Handbook, Section 340.1 . Question #8 and the data information are printed on the L/IN. If required changes are reported, proof of resource changes must be provided. Block 8V is checked if the proof of changes is inadequate or not attached, and the statement "You did not provide proof of any changes" is printed. If both Block 8 and 8V are checked, the question and the data will be printed on the L/IN. Reminder: The CAO worker must always narrate any incomplete verification. This will make it easier for CAO and Change Center staff to help recipients with any questions on what information must be provided. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008

title: 571_Appendix C
text: Click on a link to see the chart for that month. Click on a link to see the chart for that month

title: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice
text: When the household submits a complete Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN) by the extended due date, benefits are reinstated and provided no later than 10 days after the normal issuance date . (See Chapter 571, Appendix B .) The L/IN is considered timely if the form is received on or before the extended due date shown on the notice. An L/IN is complete if it meets all of the following requirements: - All questions except for #5 (only the shelter/utility part), #6, or #7 are answered completely. NOTE: Failure to answer #5 (only the shelter/utility part), #6, or #7 or to give verification for those answers does not make the L/IN form incomplete. However, for a household to receive these deductions, the questions must be answered, with verification. If the conditions are not met, the CAO must determine SNAP eligibility or the benefit amount without considering the shelter or utility, child care, or child support deduction.

title: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice
text: - The form is signed by the payee , an authorized representative (AR) , or a responsible household member. - Acceptable verification is attached for: - Gross earned income - A questionable reported change in child care expenses or expenses for the care of a sick or disabled person that results in a deduction affecting the benefit amount - A change in gross unearned income - A change in the amount of support paid to someone outside of the household - A change in residence and the resulting change in shelter/utility costs (Verification of questionable shelter/utility costs is necessary. The CAO may need to contact a third party, such as the landlord, for verification.) - a change in medical expenses - Resources that exceed $2,000 or $3,250 for non Categorically Eligible (CE) households. - Tracking of question #8 should be completed as follows: - For households that are CE, track question #8 as complete - For households that are not CE, track question #8 as incomplete if the resource or verification is not provided. Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008

title: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice
text: The CAO will determine continued eligibility and benefit amount based on the information shown on the Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN) . When an L/IN is received by the extended due date and is complete with all verification attached, the CAO will: - Determine continued eligibility and benefit amount based on the information shown on the L/IN, and establish a new SAR period. - Promptly mail a Confirming Notice to let the household know about any action taken, such as reinstatement , termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the L/IN. - Reinstate benefits by the CAO deadline for reactivating suspended cases. (This ensures that the case remains active in the CFSAR system.)

title: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice
text: - Return all original verification to the household. Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes. When the CAO receives a L/IN before the extended due date but not all questions are answered completely or verification is not attached, the CAO will: - Record in the case record that benefits are terminated because the household failed to complete the L/IN. - The L/IN serves as a confirming notice, an additional confirming notice is not required to be sent. - If the missing information is provided, check for good cause: - record the good-cause decision in the case record, and take the proper action based on good cause. Reinstate the case if benefits were suspended.

title: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice
text: - record the good-cause decision in the case record, and - take the proper action based on good cause. Reinstate the case if benefits were suspended. The CAO will reinstate benefits if a complete SAR form or L/IN is received after the last day for system override but by the extended due date. NOTE: The CAO will send a Confirming Notice to let the household know about the actions to be taken. When an L/IN is not received by the extended due date, the CAO will: - Send no further notice of termination. (The L/IN serves as notice of closing.) - Record in the case record that benefits are terminated because the household failed to complete an L/IN on time.

title: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice
section_title: 571.64 L/IN Not Received by the Extended Due Date
text: - Record the good-cause decision in the case record, and take the proper action based on good cause. Reinstate the case if benefits are suspended. - Record the good-cause decision in the case record, and - take the proper action based on good cause. Reinstate the case if benefits are suspended. - Review the weekly activity report for cases suspended by CFSAR because the household did not turn in the L/IN on time. Close the case if it is appropriate.

title: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or
section_title: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or
text: The CFSAR system will suspend benefits for any household that does not submit either a SAR form or complete L/IN by the extended due date, no notice is required. If there is no response to the L/IN, the CAO will record in the case record that the case is suspended. - Do not send notification of termination of benefits as the L/IN serves as the notice of closing. - If the case is reinstated due to good cause , the CAO will record the good-cause reason in the case record. - If the case is later reinstated after the CFSAR update deadline, the CAO will enter the correct CFSAR code to reenroll the case in the CFSAR system. Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008

title: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and
section_title: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and
text: The CAO will not suspend benefits if the information shown on the SAR form or the L/IN shows ineligibility that is expected to last for one month only. Example: A household submits a SAR form in July saying the household will receive a one-time payment from a relative in August. The payment, combined with other income, makes the household ineligible for one month. The CAO will not suspend benefits. The household is notified of the zero issuance for August and the continuation of benefits beginning in September. The CAO must remove the income for September. When a household submits a SAR form showing it received income that is not expected to last, the CAO will average only the income that is expected to continue during the semiannual period.

title: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and
section_title: 571.81 Processing one Month of Zero Issuance or a Temporary Increase in Income
text: Example: A household submits a SAR form in January and provides all pay stubs for December. The person got overtime pay in the holiday season. The overtime is not expected to continue. The CAO will not average the overtime pay when determining the income to adjust for the next SAR period. The CAO will terminate a household's benefits if the information shown on the SAR form or L/IN shows ineligibility that is expected to last for more than one month. The CAO will send a Confirming Notice to the household. The household must submit a new application if benefits are terminated because of eligibility criteria such as excess income and/or resources.

title: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and
text: The CAO will not require a new application if the household reapplies within 30 days of the closing and/or complies with the requirement (including SAR) that originally closed the case. SNAP benefits will be prorated to the date the household provides the information and verification. The renewal date will remain the same as previously established in e CIS . However, the household had the option to submit a new application. If the CAO receives a new application, the CAO will review it for expedited services. The SAR form is not required for processing expedited services or ongoing benefits. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008

title: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process
text: Households required to participate in SAR may appeal any adverse action within 90 days from the action or loss of benefits. (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 870 .) Benefits will continue until the end of the certification period or until the hearing result if the appeal challenges an action based on noncompliance with SAR requirements and the household claims it submitted a SAR form or a L/IN . NOTE: The household must complete a SAR form for the reporting period in question. The CAO will not continue benefits if the appeal is based on the requirement to participate in SAR, and the household does not complete the SAR form. The CAO will change the benefit amount using information provided on the SAR form, except for factors that are the reason for the appeal. Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008

title: 572.1 General Policy
text: State and federal governments periodically make changes that affect an entire caseload or part of a caseload. These are known as mass changes . They affect the following: - SNAP changes - Public Assistance ( TANF / GA ) grant changes - Federal benefit changes Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 26, 2004

title: 572.2 SNAP Changes
text: SNAP changes include adjustments to the following: - Income eligibility standards (gross/net income test and eligibility limit) - Thrifty Food Plan (maximum benefit ) The CAO does not need to notify the household of mass changes in SNAP. The system will issue a notice to the household. 7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)(ii)

title: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes
text: Public assistance grant changes include changes to the following: - Benefit level When an overall adjustment is made to TANF / GA grants, a household receiving TANF/GA and SNAP benefits must have the new grant amount applied to its SNAP budget. If the CAO is notified of the TANF/GA grant change at least 30 days in advance, SNAP benefits must be changed in the same month the grant change takes effect. When the CAO is not given sufficient notice, the change must be made no later than the month following the month of the TANF/GA grant change. For most budgets, the Client Information System (CIS) will apply the new grant amount, compute the benefit amount, let the household know about the change, and send a notice to the case worker for control.

title: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes
text: The CAO must manually compute cases that cannot be processed by the system. The CAO must: - Recalculate the benefit amount using the new grant amount; and - Notify the household of the change, using a Confirming Notice ( PA/FS 162-C ). Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing April 16, 2008

title: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes
text: Federal changes include periodic cost-of-living adjustments (COLAs) and other mass changes to the following: - Social Security Retirement, Survivor's, and Disability Insurance (RSDI) - Supplemental Security Income (SSI) When there is a COLA or other mass change in federal benefits: - SAR households do not have to report mass grant changes unless the mass grant change causes the total gross household income to go above the 130% FPIG limit. - Non-SAR households with unearned income have to report if the mass grant change causes the householdâs gross monthly unearned income to change by more than $50. When information about a mass change is not available through the I ncome Eligibility Verification System (IEVS), such as for VA, Black Lung, and Railroad Retirement benefits, the CAO must contact the household to determine whether there is an income change.

title: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes
text: The CAO must adjust the benefit amount when it learns about a mass change. The CAO must change the benefit amount by the time the second benefit is issued after the month in which the change occurred. If the household's certification period ends before issuance of the second benefit, the adjustment must be made at renewal . The CAO must verify mass changes in RSDI benefits through BENDEX , State Data Exchange (SDX), or IEVS. VA, Railroad Retirement, and Black Lung information is not available through system matches. Households receiving these benefits may or may not receive the COLA. The information must be verified case by case.

title: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes
text: A household not required to be enrolled in SAR does not need to provide verification until the next renewal if there is an unearned-income change of $50 or less. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing April 16, 2008

title: 572.5 Notice
text: The CAO must send a Confirming Notice ( PA/FS 162-C ) to notify the household of a mass change in the cash grant or federal benefits. The CAO must take the following actions: - Send a PA/FS 162-C to SNAP-only households when increasing, decreasing, or closing benefits during the certification period . - Send a PA/FS 162-C to combination cash assistance/SNAP or Medical Assistance (MA)/SNAP households when a PA/FS 162-A is not required to decrease or close cash assistance or MA benefits. - Send the PA/FS 162-C so that the household receives it no later than the day the household receives the benefit. This applies to all households, regardless of how they are to report changes. - If the change in benefits is made at renewal , send a Notice to Applicant ( PA/FS 162 ). The household must receive the notice no later than the end of the current certification period. NOTE: If an Advance Notice ( PA/FS 162-A ) is sent to let a household know about an adverse action to cash assistance or MA benefits (or both), the same notice can be used for a change in the benefit amount. The CAO must send the PA/FS 162-A so that the household will receive the notice no later than the day the benefit is received. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing April 16, 2008

title: 572.6 Fair Hearing
text: A household has the right to request a fair hearing when benefits are adjusted because of a mass change . If the appeal is filed timely, the CAO must not take action to reduce or terminate benefits until the hearing decision is received. Benefits continue at their previous level if: - A fair hearing is requested timely; - The right to continued benefits is not waived; and - The fair hearing is based on an improper computation of benefits or improper application or interpretation of federal laws or regulations. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 26, 2004

title: 575.1 General Policy
text: Elderly/Disabled Simplified Application Process (ESAP) Certification Period , PFS-22450-575 (Published March 13, 2026) The CAO must assign a certification period once it determines that a household is eligible to participate in SNAP. A certification period is the period of time within which a household is eligible to receive benefits. The CAO assigns the longest certification period possible based on the household's circumstances and characteristics. See Section 575.2 . NOTE: The expedited and ongoing certification periods may be authorized separately and considered separate certification periods. SNAPH 575.1 advises that the CAO must assign a certification period once it determines that a household is eligible to participate in SNAP. Following the expedited OTI, the ongoing SNAP determination was pended for verification. Once the verification is received and the eligibility determined, the certification period is assigned based on the beginning date of the ongoing benefit. Example: A household applies on 5/17/24 and is determined to be eligible for expedited benefits, but also reports income changes which must be verified for ongoing benefits. The expedited benefits are issued for 5/17/24-6/30/24 in a 2-month certification period and the case is pended for income verification for the ongoing benefit. When the verification is received, the case is processed with a begin date of 7/1/24 for the ongoing benefit, so the certification period for the ongoing benefit will expire on 6/30/25 if the household qualifies for the standard 12-month certification period, 6/30/26 if they qualify for a 24-month certification period or 6/30/27 for FS75 cases. SNAP benefits stop at the end of each certification period. The CAO continues benefits only after renewal of eligibility. See Chapter 576, Renewal . The household must receive written notice of the begin and end dates of the certification period. See Section 575.4.

title: 575.1 General Policy
text: The certification period is based on calendar months. It begins the first day of the first month of eligibility. - A household applies in January and is eligible for benefits in January. The application is processed in February. The certification period begins in January. - A household applies in January and is ineligible for benefits in January. The CAO determines the householdâs SNAP eligibility begins in February and the certification period begins in February. An eligible household that is not receiving a benefit because of proration is considered eligible in that month. The month that the benefits are prorated to $0 is the first month of the certification period.

title: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods
text: The CAO must assign the longest certification period possible, using the guidelines below. NOTE: The CAO must not base the certification period on the household 's enrollment in SAR . For SAR policy relating to certification periods, see Chapter 571.11 . 575.21 Households Assigned a Thirty-Six Month Certification Period The CAO may assign a 36 month certification period to the household if: - All members are elderly or disabled and - Have countable unearned income only NOTE: These budgets are signified by budget and program status code of FS 75 NOTE: If these households have zero income because they are part of the Elderly/Disabled Simplified Application Process(ESAP) they should receive a 36 month certification period.

title: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods
section_title: 575.22 Households Assigned a Twenty-Four Month Certification Period
text: The CAO may assign 24-month certification period to the household if : - All adult members are elderly or disabled with countable income . For the definition of elderly/disabled, see Chapter 510.21 Definitions of Elderly and Disabled individuals. NOTE: If all adults in the household are elderly or disabled, they may receive the 24-month certification period even if they have earned income; however, they must be assigned a 6-month certification period if they have zero income as indicated below. The CAO may assign a 12-month certification period if the household has: - Earned income, or - Unearned income. - All adult members are not elderly or disabled If the household no longer receives earned or unearned income, the certification period does not change.

title: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods
section_title: 575.23 Households Assigned a Twelve-Month Certification Period
text: The CAO may also assign a 12-month certification period to a Pennsylvanians with Employment and/or Engagement Requirements (PEER) household that is not subject to time limits if the household lives in a geographically waived area. See Chapter 575.25 . NOTE: Households whose income ends in the initial month and zero income is being counted for ongoing benefits are considered a zero-income household as indicated in Section 575.24 and should receive the appropriate certification period of 6-months or less depending on PEER status. The CAO must assign a six-month certification period to the household if it is: - A migrant or seasonal farm worker household;

title: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods
section_title: 575.24 Households Assigned a Certification Period of Six Months
text: - A zero-income household unless an ESAP (FS75) household; or - Has only earned or unearned income that is exempt. NOTE: The CAO must consider a household whose only income is from cash assistance as a household with exempt unearned income. NOTE: Households which contain one or more PEER, must be assigned the certification period as indicated in Section 575.25. If the PEER is not currently meeting the work requirement and is not exempt by law, the CAO must base the length of the certification period on the number of months the PEER is allowed to receive SNAP benefits. See Chapter 536.3 .

title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
section_title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
text: When a change in circumstances requires cash assistance to stop and the CAO does not have enough information to determine the SNAP benefit amount, the CAO must take the following actions: - Send a request for clarification or proof. - If the household fails to respond to the request, send the household an Advance Notice of Adverse Action advising the household of its plans to terminate the SNAP benefits. The CAO must continue SNAP benefits during the appeal process if the household asks for a hearing on the proposed plan to stop cash assistance benefits. The CAO must align all budgets that can be aligned at the next application, SAR review, renewal or reported change that results in an eligibility determination. See Appendix C for general guidelines to follow when aligning budgets within a case record.

title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
text: NOTE: The renewal year for a 36-month ESAP budget will never align with the renewal year of a 12-month MA or TANF budget, however the CAO should still align the renewal month so the client recipient receives their notices for all budgets at the same time (the ESAP interim contact letter PA1937 and the MA/TANF renewal packets). If the recipient fails to complete their next MA/TANF renewal, those budgets can close and the recipient will still have the remaining two years of their three-year ESAP certification, thus getting their full certification period. When a SNAP household enrolled in SAR applies for Cash or Medical Assistance (MA) using an application form ( PA 600 or PA 600R ) that includes SNAP, the CAO must align the renewal dates. Exception: Alignment policy does not apply to Extended SNAP (FS 43) households. NOTE: Remember that MA may be opened with a renewal period of less than 12 months. SNAP certification periods may not always have to be shortened to align renewal periods for all budgets. The CAO may shorten the SNAP certification period by completing a renewal interview. If the household does not cooperate in providing the necessary information to complete the renewal, the CAO must close the budget. NOTE: If the SNAP packet was mailed and not returned, the CAO will close SNAP using reason code 042 After the renewal, the CAO must: - Assign a new certification period that aligns with the Cash or MA renewal period. - Send a confirming notice to the household stating the benefit amount (even if it has not changed) and new certification period. The CAO is not required to issue supplemental benefits or submit an overpayment referral after completing the renewal if: - The individual does not submit verification of required information within 30 days but does provide it within 60 days

title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
text: - The CAO does not process the Cash or MA application timely. If a household files an appeal within 15 days of the date the Confirming Notice is mailed because the CAO proposes to decrease SNAP benefits and elects to continue to receive the higher benefit amount, the CAO must continue the original benefit amount until the hearing decision or the certification period ends, whichever is earlier. The budgets cannot be aligned. If the household appeals after 15 days but before 90 days, benefits continue in the reduced amount until the hearing decision. Budgets can be aligned. The CAO must narrate all eligibility decisions and case actions made at application, SAR, renewal, reported change and verbal or written contact with the household. For renewals, the CAP must narrate how the renewal was conducted (face-to-face, mail, telephone). Case comments must also include the statement that data exchanges (DX) were reviewed for all individuals who may affect the households eligibility. 1. A SNAP-only SAR household has a September 2025 through August 2026 certification period. The household's circumstances change and the household applies for and is determined eligible for MA in December 2025 . The CAO completes the SNAP renewal at the MA interview. Since the SNAP-only SAR household is now eligible for MA, the SNAP certification period is shortened and now runs December 2025 through November 2026 . The SNAP and MA SAR review date is May 2026 . The household's SNAP benefits are reduced as a result of the information provided on the application for Medicaid. The CAO sends the household a Confirming Notice using Option 6 informing it of the new benefit amount and new certification period. 2. A SNAP-only SAR household has a July 2025 through June 2026 certification period. The household's circumstances change. The household applies for Cash Assistance and is determined ineligible. The SNAP certification period does not change.

title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
text: 3. A SNAP / Medicaid SAR household has a July 2025 through June 2026 certification period. The households circumstances change. The household applies for and is authorized Cash Assistance effective February 2026 with a renewal date of February 2027 . The CAO completes a SNAP renewal at the Cash Assistance interview. The household's new SNAP certification period is March 2026 through February 2027 . 4. A SNAP/MA household has a certification period of August 2025 through July 2026 . The household applies for Cash Assistance on February 14, 2026 . The CAO denies the application because the household does not provide required verification within 30 days. On March 17th, the household provides all verification. The CAO authorizes Cash Assistance, setting a renewal date of March 16, 2027 . The CAO completes a SNAP renewal new SNAP certification period is April 2026 through March 2027 . The SNAP and Cash Assistance SAR due date is October 2026 . The CAO does not issue supplemental SNAP benefits or compete an overpayment referral for March 2026 .

title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
section_title: 575.32 Â System Process for Shortening the SNAP Certification Period
text: When opening a Cash Assistance or MA budget, the CAO must: - Process the case as a Program Add and be sure to select the appropriate method of verification for each item, - Enter all mandatory (*) fields on any additional screens that appear, - Update any necessary Individual Non-financial, Resource or Income information until the Execute Eligibility screen is presented. Click "Run Eligibility" button, and proceed through the subsequent screens, - At the SNAP Budget Authorization Screen, amend the SAR Due Date and the Reapp Due Date fields, - At the Post Authorization/Client Notice screen, ensure or change the FS00 notice to Notice 001, option 6, - Continue processing through the case and complete all mandatory (*) fields. Submit the case, - If the existing SNAP budget does not change as a result of the addition of the Cash or Medical Assistance budgets, the system will not issue Notice 001. Issue a manual notice to inform the household of the revised certification period, - Ensure comments are added to the case narrate to reflect the changed dates.

title: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
section_title: 575.33 Â System Process for Updating a Renewal or SAR
text: - On the Case Summary Screen, select "Maintenance" from the "What would you like to do today?" drop down and click the "Next" button. - On the Left-Hand Navigation bar, click on "Execute Eligibility", - After eligibility has run, click the "Next" button, - The next screen will be the "Eligibility Results" screen. Ensure the benefits are correct and then click the "Next" button. - On the "Budget Authorization" screen, click on the calendar or manually type in the SAR Due Date under SAR Due Date, then click on the "Next" button, - Click on the calendar or manually type in the Reapplication Due Date under Reapp due Date and then click on the "Next" button, - Continue through Case Processing, entering all relevant information to the case and completing all mandatory (*) fields through case commit. and FNS waiver #2030048, granted under the authority of 7 CFR 272.3(c)(1)(ii)

title: 575.4 Certification Notices
text: The CAO must provide the household with a Notice of Eligibility listing the begin and end dates of the certification period. For an initial application, the CAO must provide the notice as soon as the eligibility determination is made, no later than 30 days from the application date. For a renewal , the CAO must provide the notice before the end of the current certification period . In expedited cases when the certification period is longer than one month and verification is pending, the notice must explain that benefits will stop if verification is not provided. The system will issue the PA 10-SP before the end of the certification period for any household with a certification period of three months or more.

title: 575_Appendix A
text: Type of Household or Income Earned and/or unearned income, all adult members are not elderly or disabled All adults are elderly/disabled and there may be earned income on the case Unearned income only and all household members are elderly/disabled (ESAP FS 75)** Zero-income households C = Certification R = Renewal * = Must be provided to clients who are nonexempt from work registration (see Chapter 535.3 ). It is not necessary to obtain a signature. ** = a PA 1935 can be used to apply for ESAP and a PA 1939 should be sent to renew for ESAP. NOTE : Other specialized forms may be necessary.

title: 576.1 General Policy
text: S upplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Closing SNAP Benefits before the end of the Certification Period when Renewal is not Complete, PFS-21170-576 (Published November 16, 2023) TANF and SNAP Processing during the COVID-19 Emergency , PFS-19846-576 (Published April 15, 2020) The CAO is responsible for completing, processing, and approving or denying applications for renewal . The CAO must complete the renewal process before the end of a household 's current certification period . The household must complete the renewal process timely to receive uninterrupted benefits. If the time frame for submitting verification extends beyond the certification period, the CAO cannot complete a renewal until the verification is received. The CAO must provide uninterrupted benefits within five working days if verification is received after the end of the certification period but within the 30-day time frame. If the renewal process is not completed before the end of the certification period, benefits will automatically stop. The CAO must send a Confirming Notice telling the household that benefits have stopped. A household that is receiving Extended SNAP benefits is not renewed until the Extended SNAP period ends or the household believes it may receive more SNAP benefits because of changes in household circumstances. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing May 3, 2007

title: 576.2 Notice of Expiration
text: The CAO will receive a system -generated Notice of Expiration ( PA 10-SP ) for each household for which certification is about to end. The system will send the PA 10-SP to the CAO before the last month of the household's certification period . The CAO must mail the PA 10-SP , a renewal form, and an Appointment Notice and Verification Checklist ( PA 253 ) to the household at least one day before the last month of the certification period. The system will not generate a PA 10-SP for households certified for only one or two months. The SNAP renewal for a combination cash assistance/SNAP household is completed at the same time the cash assistance renewal is completed. The PA 10-SP is not required if the joint cash assistance/SNAP renewal is completed within the certification period. For a household certified for only one or two months, the CAO must prepare the PA 10-SP and give it to the household, along with a renewal form, at the time of certification.

title: 576.2 Notice of Expiration
section_title: 576.21 Completion of the Notice of Expiration
text: The PA 10-SP generated by the system or initiated by the CAO must contain the following information: - Household name and address - CAO name, address, and phone number The CAO needs to complete only the date and time of the interview for PA 10-SP s generated by the system. For all PA 10-SP s, the original goes to the household, and the copy stays in the case record for three years. The CAO must waive the required face-to-face office interview for SSI households at renewal. It is not necessary to assess the household hardship factors listed in Section 504.43 . The CAO must routinely complete telephone interview renewals for SSI households as follows:

title: 576.2 Notice of Expiration
section_title: 576.22 Notice of Expiration and Addendum: Scheduling Telephone Interviews for SSI Households
text: 1. Mail to the SSI household a PA 10-SP , a Telephone Interview Instructions for SSI Recipients Reapplying for SNAP ( PA 10-SP Addendum ), a renewal form, and an Appointment Notice and Verification Checklist ( PA 253 ), if needed, along with a return envelope. 2. Receive the completed, signed renewal form and any required verifications from the household before the scheduled telephone interview, as instructed on the PA 10-SP Addendum . The CAO may need to request additional verification items during the renewal telephone interview. 3. Telephone the SSI head of household or AR on the day and time shown on the PA 10-SP Addendum . Conduct the renewal telephone interview. The CAO may choose to do a face-to-face office or home-visit interview for renewal for SSI households with questionable circumstances. The household may also request a face-to-face renewal interview to be completed at the CAO or the client's home. The SSI household may also choose to apply at a Social Security Administration office. The PA 10-SP Addendum copy, like the PA 10-SP copy, must be kept in the case record for three years. Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing May 3, 2007

title: 576.3 Timely Renewals
text: The household completes the application process when it does all of the following: - Submits requested verification within 30 days from the request date The CAO must process a timely application for renewal and provide uninterrupted benefits to any household found eligible. (See Section 576.31 .) - A semiannual review (SAR) household must submit a renewal form on or before the due date for the last month of certification. Example: A household's certification period ends June 6 in issuance cycle 7. The household must submit a renewal form before the SAR form due date of June 16. - A SAR household must submit a renewal form by the 15th day of the last month of certification. Example: A household's certification period ends June 6 in issuance cycle 7. The household must submit a renewal form by June 15. - A SAR household certified for only one month must submit a renewal form within 15 days from the receipt of the PA 10-SP . Example: A household certified for one month from March 7 to March 31 receives the PA 10-SP on March 5. It must submit a renewal form by March 20. - A non-SAR household certified for two months must submit a renewal form by the 15th day of the second month if the case was certified in the first month. Example: A household is certified on April 8 for two months, April and May. It receives the PA 10-SP on April 8. The household must submit a renewal form by May 15. - If the case was certified in the second month, a non-SAR household certified for two months must submit a renewal form within 15 days from the date the PA 10-SP is received. Example: A household is certified for two months, April and May, but the certification process is not completed until May 3. The household receives the PA 10-SP on May 3. It must submit a renewal form by May 18.

title: 576.3 Timely Renewals
text: The CAO must allow the household 30 calendar days to submit required verification. The CAO must begin the 30-day count with the day after the initial request for verification. The CAO must provide uninterrupted benefits to households that file timely and provide verification within 30 days. (See Section 576.31 .) The CAO must tell the household the verification requirements and record the request for verification in the case record. Example: A household files a timely renewal form on March 14 and is interviewed on March 25. Additional verification is needed. The 30-day time frame is set to end April 24. The household provides the required verification on March 29. The household is entitled to uninterrupted benefits.

title: 576.3 Timely Renewals
text: If a household (a) has been certified for one month or (b) has been certified for two months and is in the second month of the certification period, and if the household completes the renewal process timely and is found eligible, the CAO must take the following actions: - Authorize benefits so that SNAP benefits are received no later than 30 calendar days after the date the household received its last benefit. - Send a Notice of Eligibility telling the household the benefit amount and the certification period length. Example: A household is certified for one month only and receives April's benefit and a PA 10-SP on April 2. The renewal form was received timely, the interview was held, verification was provided on April 15, and the household was found eligible. The household must receive a notice and its SNAP benefits before May 2. For all other households that complete the renewal process timely and are found eligible, the CAO must take the following actions: - Authorize benefits so that the household receives SNAP benefits by the normal issuance cycle in the month after the last month of its current certification period. NOTE: If this time frame cannot be met within the period allowed for submitting verification, the CAO must authorize SNAP benefits so that they are received within five days from the date the household provided the needed verification. The CAO must not prorate benefits. - Send a notice of eligibility telling the household the benefit amount and the new certification period. Example: A household's certification period ends in November. The renewal form was received timely, the interview was held, verification was provided, and the household was found eligible. The household must receive a notice before November 30. The household is entitled to uninterrupted benefits for December. - At the end of the renewal interview, offer the client the opportunity to register to vote. If the client wishes to register, provide a registration form and help the client complete the form if help is needed. (See Supplemental Handbook Chapter 980 . )

title: 576.3 Timely Renewals
text: OPS040503 PA 600AAB for the Food Stamp Program (Linked March 12, 2008) Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing June 6, 2008

title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
section_title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
text: PFS-17721-506; Expedited SNAP benefits review for untimely SNAP renewals; October 2015 PFS12979576 Untimely Recertification Issues (Linked June 14, 2006) PFS13048576 Renewal Form Received After Closing (Linked June 14, 2006) OPS070214 Notice of Missed Interview (Linked March 12, 2008 ) OPS060403 Application Processing for Households and Families Recently Discontinued or Denied Benefits (Linked February 20, 2008) PFS13000504 Application Processing Issues - OPS-06-04-03 (Linked February 20, 2008) PFS13039504 Application After Closing/New Application Form (Linked February 20, 2008) PFS13622576 Notice Of Missed Interview Language (Linked May 19, 2007) PFS13664576 Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI) at Renewal (Linked May 15, 2007) The household has not completed the renewal process timely if it: - Fails to submit a timely renewal form; - Submits a renewal form after the certification period ends; - Submits a renewal form timely but does not complete the interview scheduled by the CAO ; or - Submits a renewal form timely and completes the interview but fails or refuses to provide requested verification within the 30-day time frame. If the household fails to submit a timely renewal form, the CAO must authorize SNAP benefits to an eligible household so that they are received no later than 30 calendar days after an untimely renewal form has been filed. (See Section 576.31 .) An untimely renewal interview will result in the proration of benefits similar to the initial application, except for migrant and seasonal farm workers. All other application procedures apply. (See Chapter 504 .) Example: A household's certification period ends September 30. The household fails to keep the scheduled renewal interview on September 11. The CAO sends a confirming notice on October 1 telling the household that benefits have been stopped. The household does not submit a renewal form until October 4. If the interview is completed by October 7, all verification is received, and the household is found eligible, then the CAO issues prorated benefits effective October 4.

title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
section_title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
text: When the household requests that benefits be reopened, asks for a new application, or submits needed verification within 30 days following the date benefits were closed, the CAO must reopen the case and re-determine SNAP eligibility without requiring a new application or renewal form if already submitted, ( see Section 579.3 ). Once the certification period has ended and there has been a full month before the CAO receives a renewal form, the CAO must require a new application to be completed. If the household submits a renewal form before an application form, the CAO must use the renewal form receipt date to calculate benefits. The CAO must attach the renewal form to the new application for auditing purposes. Example: A household's certification period ends July 31. The household fails to keep the scheduled renewal interview on July 11. The CAO sends a Confirming Notice on August 1, telling the household that benefits have been stopped. The household submits a renewal form on September 6. If a new application form is completed, the interview is completed, all verification is received, and the household is found eligible, then the CAO issues prorated benefits from September 6. If the household submits the renewal form timely but then fails to cooperate with the interview or verification requirements before the end of the certification period, the CAO must not provide uninterrupted benefits unless there has been an administrative error. If the household completes the interview or provides needed verification within 30 days of the end of the certification period, the CAO must reopen SNAP without requiring a new application ( see Section 579.3 ) . Example: A household goes to its renewal interview on September 6 but fails to provide needed verification within the allowable time frame. The CAO sends a denial notice on October 5. The verification is provided on October 17. The CAO issues prorated benefits effective October 17 if a new application form is completed.

title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
section_title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
text: If the household provides the verification after the 30-day time frame but before the end of the certification period, the CAO must provide uninterrupted benefits. Example: A household's certification period ends in May and is in issuance cycle 7. On May 5, the household completes an application for renewal. The household is found eligible, but additional verification is needed. The time frame for providing the verification ends June 3. Verification is provided on May 24. The CAO authorizes uninterrupted benefits. If an eligible household cannot participate within 30 calendar days after the application filing date because of the time allowed for submitting missing verification, the CAO must give the household an opportunity to participate within 5 work days after the household supplies the missing verification. Example: A householdâs certification period ends in April. The household keeps the scheduled application for renewal on April 1. The CAO worker starts to process the renewal with an issuance cycle 1 on April 20 and discovers that verification is needed. The CAO contacts the household on April 20 to request the verification by May 20. The household provides the verification on April 30. Because April 30 is the 30th day and benefits cannot be processed by that date, the CAO provides benefits by May 5. Benefits for May are not prorated.

title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
section_title: 576.41 Authorizing Untimely Applications, Interviews, and Verification
text: Although a household may lose its right to uninterrupted benefits, benefits must not be denied. The CAO must issue benefits within 30 days from the application date once the household complies and is otherwise eligible. When the household reapplies within 30 days of benefits closure, the CAO must reopen the case without requiring a new application or renewal form if already submitted (see Section 579.3). A household that is ineligible for benefits because it failed to meet renewal requirements must receive a denial notice before the end of the current certification period. The household must receive the denial notice no later than 30 days after the application or verification request if time frames for providing verification expire after the end of the certification period.

title: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
text: Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing May 3, 2007

title: 576.5 Failure of the County Assistance Office to Act
section_title: 576.5 Failure of the County Assistance Office to Act
text: The CAO must restore benefits lost because of an administrative error if the household completes the renewal process before the end of the certification or within 30 days after the end of the certification period (see Section 579.3). Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing May 3, 2007

title: 577.1 General Policy
text: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) On-Demand Language on the PA 253, PFS20792577 (Published February 23, 2022) Adverse Action and Advance notice for SNAP recipients, PFS18931577 (Published July 11, 2018) The CAO must give every applicant and recipient household a written notice whenever it takes any action regarding eligibility or benefit amount. All notices must include, in language the household understands, the following: - The Public Law, 7 CFR, or 55 Pa. Code citations supporting the action, shown as P.L. xxx-xxx , 7 CFR 27 x.xxx, or 55 Pa. Code 501. xx (The CAO must not use a SNAP Handbook section number as a citation.) - The householdâs right to appeal any action taken by asking for a fair hearing - The telephone number of the SNAP office - The name, address, and telephone number of any person or organization that provides free legal services The CAO must keep a copy of the paper or electronic notice for three years from the date it was sent. Manual PA/FS 162 notices must include the mailing date, which is the first business day following the processing of the case record. PFS14486577 Stockpiled Manual Notices (Linked December 9, 2008)

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
text: The CAO must send each household a completed Notice of Eligibility whenever: - An application is approved; - An application is denied; - An application is pending CAO action (see Section 577.23 ); or - A new certification period is set. The notice must be mailed no later than 30 days after the application date. When an application is approved, the notice of eligibility must include the following: - The benefit amount - The beginning and ending dates of the certification period - An explanation of benefits, if the first issuance covers more than one month - The name and telephone number of the person or office to contact for more information or to ask for a fair hearing

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
text: - An explanation of the benefit that will be issued during the certification period, when changes are anticipated The CAO may also include the following in the notice: - A reminder of the household's obligation to report changes - A reminder of the need to reapply at the end of the certification period When a household is eligible for expedited service but must give verification to qualify for benefits beyond the first month, the notice must also contain a statement that the CAO will not issue the second month's benefits until verification is provided. If verification is not provided within 60 days after receipt of the application, benefits will end and the household must reapply.

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
text: If a household is assigned a limited certification period, the CAO must include a Notice of Expiration ( PA 10-SFSP ) with the eligibility notice. When an application is denied, the notice must contain the following: - An explanation of the reason for the denial, including the regulations on which the denial was based and the federal or state citation for those regulations If a household is denied benefits because it failed to complete the application process, the CAO must send the denial notice on the 30th day. The 30-day count begins the day after the application is received. The denial notice must have the following information in addition to the information listed above:

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
section_title: 577.221 Failure to Complete the Application Process
text: - A statement that the case will be authorized without a new application if the client provides the needed verification within 30 days of the denial notice mailing date - A statement that a new application will be required if the household does not provide the requested additional information by the end of the 30-day period The CAO must give the household at least 10 days from the initial request date to provide verification. The 10 days may exceed the 30-day period. If a household is denied benefits because it refused to provide required information or verification, the denial notice must have the following information in addition to the information listed above:

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
section_title: 577.222 Refusal to provide required information or verification
text: - A statement of what information or verification the household did not provide - A statement of what action the household did not take - The federal or state citation for the regulation that requires the information - If applicable, a statement of what the worker did to help the household get the information When an application is held because of a CAO delay, the notice must contain an explanation of the pending status. It must be sent by the 30th day. When an application is held because of a CAO delay and some action is needed by the household, the notice must contain the following: - A statement that failure to comply with the action within 60 days of the application filing date will result in denial of the application

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
text: See Chapter 504 to determine the difference between CAO delay requiring a pending notice and household delay requiring a denial notice. The CAO must send a notice when a new certification period is set. The CAO must send the eligibility or denial notice by the end of the certification period if the application for certification is filed by the 15th day of the last month of the certification period. The CAO must give a notice of eligibility or denial no later than 30 days after the last benefit to any household that received a PA 10-SFSP at renewal time and reapplied timely. The CAO must not use the PA 10-SFSP to shorten a householdâs current certification period.

title: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
text: Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

title: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action
text: Adverse Action and Advance notice for SNAP recipients, PFS-18931-577 (Published July 11, 2018) The CAO must give every household a timely and adequate Advance Notice before taking any action to reduce or end benefits within a certification period . (See Section 577.4 for actions that do not require an Advance Notice.) 7 CFR § 273.13 (a)(1) and 55 Pa. Code § 501.9(h) The CAO must mail the Advance Notice at least 15 days before the proposed effective date of the action. The day after the mailing date is the first day of the 15 -day time period. This 15 -day period gives the household time to exercise its right of appeal with benefits continued pending the hearing decision. If the CAO receives the hearing request on the next work day after a 15 -day period that ends on a weekend or holiday, benefits must continue pending the hearing decision. To meet required time frames, the CAO may take action before the 15 -day period ends. If action has been taken and the household appeals within 15 days and has not waived continuation, the CAO must make sure that a supplemental benefit is available to the household by the normal due date of the regular benefit. The supplemental benefit must cover the difference in benefit amounts. In addition to the information on all notices, the advance notice of adverse action must include the following: - A statement of the action to be taken - An explanation of the reason for the action, including the federal or state citation on which the action is based - A statement of the action needed to establish continued eligibility, if applicable - Information about the availability of continuing benefits if an action is appealed

title: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action
text: - The name and telephone number of the person or office to contact for more information or to ask for a fair hearing - The name, address, and telephone number of any person or organization that provides free legal services The CAO must use the Advance Notice only to propose an adverse action after a household has failed to meet a program requirement. The CAO must not use the Advance Notice to schedule an appointment or to request verification.

title: 577.4 Confirming Notice
text: The CAO must send a Confirming Notice telling the household that an action has been taken when the benefit has been increased or when the benefit has been decreased as a result of any of the following: - An oral request in the presence of the CAO or a written request signed by a household member or someone acting in the household's behalf - A report that a household member is institutionalized - A report of the death of all household members - Eligibility termination for a drug-rehabilitation-center resident or group-living-arrangement resident if the facility loses its state certification or FNS disqualifies it as a retailer - A report from a household member or someone acting in the household's behalf that a person has moved permanently to another county - Information provided on a SAR form - Changes in income or circumstances during the certification period that were anticipated, with notice provided at the time of certification - A SNAP mass change , such as an increase or decrease in the standard deduction or standard utility allowance , if the system does not issue the notice automatically - A cash assistance program mass grant change - A federal benefit mass change - An expedited longer certification period assigned to a household with verification postponed. (The household must have received written notice that benefits could continue after the application month providing the CAO received the postponed verification and could act on it.) - A household converted from cash or SNAP benefit repayment to benefit reduction because of failure to make repayment - Increased income due to receipt of a cash grant that was not counted in the benefit (This applies to joint cash assistance/SNAP applications where the notice told the household that SNAP benefits would be reduced if cash assistance was authorized.)

title: 577.4 Confirming Notice
text: - The client's whereabouts being unknown and departmental mail (such as CAO mail or client messages) has been returned by the post office or ACCESS cards have been returned by Central Office NOTE: The CAO must try to establish, when possible, the reason for the returned mail. A client could be hospitalized or temporarily incapacitated and not be able to pick up the mail. The CAO must exercise caution before closing a case because of returned mail. The issuance date is the latest date by which a household must receive a Confirming Notice. When SNAP benefits have not been accessed in the Electronic Benefit Transfer account for 180 days, benefits are moved off line and the case is automatically suspended. The system will generate a confirming notice to the household and a copy to the CAO. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

title: 577_Appendix A
text: Notice to ApplicantâEligible Notice to ApplicantâNot Eligible Right to Appeal and Fair Hearing Verification Request Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

title: 577_Appendix B
section_title: CM 430, âSupplemental Nutrition Assistance Program Income Reporting Informationâ
text: CM 430 explains reporting requirements for households enrolled in semiannual reporting (SAR). SAR households are required to report when the gross monthly income exceeds 130% of the federal poverty income guidelines (FPIG) amount. The message contains a chart that displays the 130% FPIG amount for various household sizes. Each October, the United States Department of Agriculture mandates a change to the FPIG. CM 430 is mailed annually to SNAP-only and SNAP/Medical Assistance (MA) households enrolled in SAR. CM 430 is available in English and Spanish. Click here for the English CM 430. Click here for the Spanish CM 430.

title: 577.5 Client Verification Request Form (PA 162-VR)
text: The CAO must send the Client Verifications Requirements ( PA 162VR ) allowing the household 10 calendar days to resolve conflicting information when: - The CAO discovers that a household has not reported information that appears on the Income Eligibility Verification System (IEVS); or - The CAO discovers that information from IEVS conflicts with statements made by the household. If the household refuses or fails to cooperate in resolving the conflict, the CAO must propose to stop benefits by sending an Advance Notice of Adverse Action. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

title: 577.6 Notice of Expiration (PA 10-SFSP)
text: Each household will receive a Notice of Expiration ( PA 10-SFSP ) in the automated renewal packet. The CAO must enter the date and time of the interview on the PA 10-SFSP during the automated renewal process. A PA 10-SFSP is given at the time of renewal to a household that is certified for only one or two months. It may not be used to shorten a householdâs current certification period . The CAO must provide each household with a PA 10-SFSP during the month before the last month of the certification period. This notice must not be sent earlier than the month before the last month of the certification period. When a cash renewal is due and the household also receives SNAP benefits, the CAO must not shorten the SNAP renewal period. The CAO must process the renewal for SNAP, suppress the notice, and reset the SNAP renewal date to the original renewal due date. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

title: 577.7 Notice of Denial of Expedited Service (PA/FS 163)
section_title: 577.7 Notice of Denial of Expedited Service (PA/FS 163)
text: When an applicant household cannot be contacted in person or by telephone to be told of the expedited service denial, the CAO must mail the Notice of Denial of Expedited Service ( PA/FS 163 ) to the household. The PA/FS 163 notice must be mailed on the same day the CAO determines that the household is ineligible for expedited service. The CAO must document that the household was informed of the denial. It is not necessary to maintain a copy of the PA/FS 163 in the case file. The PA/FS 163 must not be substituted for the Notice of Eligibility ( PA/FS 162 ) that informs the household of ineligibility to receive SNAP benefits. Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

title: 578.1 General Policy
text: PFS-22222-578 National Accuracy Clearinghouse (NAC) Alert Disposition Direction (Published July 22, 2025) PFS-21946-578 Social Security Administration (SSA) Requesting Confirmation of Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Enrollment, Issued October 21, 2024 PFS-20723-578 The use of Social Media to verify Applicant/Recipient Information, Issued December 27, 2021 PFS-19818-578 Guidance on Verification Requirements during the COVID-19 emergency, Issued May 22, 2020 PFS-18914-578 Change to ARRC 123 alert due date Issued September 4, 2018 PFS-17638-578 SNAP Verification Requests and Rejections Revised July 30, 2015 Every household is required, as a condition of eligibility, to prove certain information about its circumstances. Sources of verification include the following: See Chapter 578, Appendix A for a list of generally available verification sources.

title: 578.2 Household Responsibility
text: The household has primary responsibility for providing proof (documentary evidence) to support statements on the application and to resolve any questionable information. The household's statements about their circumstances is not required verification in most instances. A person can give written evidence to the CAO by mail, by fax, by e-mail, or through an authorized representative. The household may substitute a collateral contact for written evidence. The household is responsible for naming the collateral contact. The CAO must provide help if it is needed. A household interviewed by phone is not exempt from verification requirements. The household must provide the required verification or name collateral contacts who can verify its statements. The CAO must not require a member of the household to come to the CAO to provide verification. A household that refuses to provide required verification or fails to respond to a CAO request for verification without good cause is ineligible or does not receive the deduction, until the verification is provided. - For applicants, benefits are denied or authorized without the deduction. - For recipients, benefits are terminated following timely notice or are adjusted without the deduction. A household is required as a condition of eligibility to sign the Addendum to Application for Benefits ( AAB ) with the SNAP application and renewal forms. NOTE: AAB language has been incorporated into the COMPASS rights and responsibilities section. Administrative Notice 6-99, issued January 4, 1999 Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing October 31, 2008

title: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility
text: The CAO will provide an Appointment Notice and Verification Checklist ( PA 253 ), at application and renewal , telling the household what verification must be submitted to be certified/ recertified for SNAP benefits. The CAO will determine what information must be verified from: - Information on the application - Statements by the household during the interview NOTE: Beginning October 1, 2010, if a birth certificate is required for any reason for a Puerto Rico-born individual, CAOs must only accept birth certificates issued on or after July 1, 2010. PFS 18433-578 ; Verification of Terminated Income, April 18, 2017 PFS 18434-578 ; No Income Reported at Application and Renewal, April 18, 2017 The CAO must do the following: - Verify changes reported on the SAR form at the six-month review, if required. - Be flexible in accepting reasonable documentation from the household. - Not limit verification to a specific document or source but may accept more than one document or source for verification. This means that the CAO can ask for income verification but should not limit the acceptable type of verification to a single type of verification, such as paystubs . â¢ Example: The client indicates that they work at Giant Food Stores. The caseworker can request verification of income they receive from employment. The client provides a letter from their boss stating how much the client makes. This is acceptable verification. â¢ Example: The client states that they are receiving Social Security Disability benefits. The caseworker can request that the client provide verification of income they receive in Social Security Benefits if it canât be verified through automated sources. The client provides a bank statement with deposits that indicate their Social Security benefits. This is acceptable verification. â¢ Example: The client states that they are self-employed. The caseworker can request that the client provide verification of self-employment income by providing a copy of their tax return. The client provides copies of invoices and receipts for the past month instead. This is acceptable verification.

title: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility
text: - Not require the household to present verification in person. NOTE: An Authorization for Release of Information ( PA 4 ) is not a requirement for SNAP. A household cannot be denied benefits for failing to sign a blanket PA 4 . However, the CAO may use a PA 4 if a specific eligibility item needs a signed release form. At the initial certification interview, the household will be given a minimum of 10 days from the request date to provide missing verification. At the renewal interview, the household will be given 30 days from the request date to provide missing verification. The CAO will help the household get proof of its statements, providing the household is cooperating, as specified in Section 504.15 . T he CAO will ensure the household, (who must sign the AAB), knows the impact of not reporting and/or verifying expenses. The household must be informed of their right to report expenses to the CAO any time during the certification period . The case record must clearly establish eligibility or ineligibility and benefit amount. The CAO will: - explain in the case notes any decision to require additional verification or to verify inconsistent information. - explain actions taken in the case notes Important: Once information which is not likely to change is verified and recorded in the case record, that information does not need to be verified again. Items such as the Social Security Number (SSN), birth date, and citizenship are not normally expected to change. However, the worker may require verification again if there has been a change or the original information was not correct. The decision to re-verify must be explained in the case record.

title: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility
text: The CAO will follow cash assistance verification requirements for information that needs to be verified for both cash assistance and SNAP. If all SNAP verification requirements are met but cash requirements are not, the CAO may not delay issuing SNAP benefits beyond the 30-day application time frame. The CAO will verify the following information before authorizing benefits: - Gross nonexempt income (see Chapter 550 ) Exception: When an otherwise eligible applicant has cooperated but cannot verify income because a third party is late or not cooperating, the worker must use the best available information to authorize benefits. Reminder: Workers should check PACSES for child support or alimony (even if listed as voluntary on the application).

title: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility
text: - Noncitizen immigration status of each household member identified as a noncitizen (see Chapter 522 and Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 740 ) Exception: A noncitizen is certified up to six months until a federal agency verifies citizenship status. - Countable months of SNAP benefits received by an able-bodied adult without dependents (ABAWD) from another state - Number of hours worked by an ABAWD - The obligation to pay utility expenses to determine the appropriate standard utility allowance. Verification is only needed if the information provided by the household is questionable . (see Chapter 560 ) - The obligation to pay utility expenses for a home unoccupied because of employment or training away from home, illness, or abandonment caused by a natural disaster or casualty loss, if the costs result in a deduction (see Chapter 560 ) - Medical costs if they result in a deduction (see Chapter 560 ) - Reimbursements for deductible medical expenses (see Chapter 560 ) - SSNs reported by the household are submitted to the Social Security Administration (SSA) for verification by SSA procedures. (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 950 .) NOTE: The CAO will not delay certification for and issuance of benefits to an otherwise eligible household solely to verify the SSN of a household member. Most SSNs can be verified through the Client Information System (CIS). - Pennsylvania residence (see Chapter 523 ) Exception: The requirement to verify residence before authorization may be unreasonable in unusual cases. The CAO may postpone the requirement in situations such as homeless households, migrant or seasonal farm worker households, and households that have just moved into the state. - Identity of the person signing the application. If the application is signed by an AR, the CAO must verify the identity of both the AR and the payment name (see Chapter 520 ).

title: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility
text: NOTE: Any document that reasonably establishes identity must be accepted. The CAO will not require a specific type of document, such as a birth certificate.
